label: 獠牙凯旋怪物图鉴 pf2e.triumph-of-the-tusk-bestiary

Hold Warrior (Forge).name: 氏族勇士(锻造厂) Hold Warrior (Forge)

Ferocity.name: 凶猛 Ferocity
Ferocity.description:
触发 氏族勇士的生命值降低至0点;

效果 氏族勇士免于被打倒，并且余留1点HP，其@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2] 值增加1。当其@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{受伤 Wounded 3}的时候，它无法再使用此能力。

Tar-Baphon's Shadow.name: 塔-巴丰的魅影 Tar-Baphon's Shadow
Tar-Baphon's Shadow.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:33] (专家)来扰乱塔-巴丰的魔法, @Check[occultism|dc:30] 或 @Check[religion|dc:30] (大师) 来压制阴影的能量; 除非摧毁残躯，否则必须在上述检定中累计获得三次成功才能解除作祟，若摧毁残躯则自动解除。
Tar-Baphon's Shadow.hazarddescription: 塔-巴丰的一个化身会汲取其目标的神力精华。
Tar-Baphon's Shadow.routine: (1 动作; 死亡) 作祟会吸取扎格雷什的神力，造成@Damage[5d10[void]]伤害。

Unholy Leech.name: 邪秽寄生 Unholy Leech
Unholy Leech.description:
触发 一个生物进入洞穴

效果 魅影附着在残躯上，释放出@Template[type:emanation|distance:30] 的死灵能量。区域内的生物（残躯除外）必须进行一次 @Check[fortitude|dc:30] 豁免，否则受到 @Damage[2d12[void]] 。然后作祟进行先攻检定。

大成功 该生物不受影响。

成功 该生物受到全额伤害，并被击退 5 尺。

失败 该生物受到全额伤害，并被击退 15 尺。

大失败 如同失败，生物 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1} 并 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]。

Unholy Presence.name: 邪秽存在 Unholy Presence
Unholy Presence.description: 魅影由塔-巴丰之力维持。每轮开始时，魅影由虚能伤害修复，恢复10 点生命值。

Marguk Cleftneck.name: 玛古克·裂颈 Marguk Cleftneck
Marguk Cleftneck.hp: 再生 20 (受到火焰或电击后失效)

Independent Brains.name: 独立脑袋 Independent Brains
Independent Brains.description: 每个玛古克的头单独进行先攻判定，并拥有自己的回合。两个头都不能延后回合。在某个头的回合开始时，该头获得 2 个动作和 1 个反应。每个头控制玛古克的一只手臂，但两只头都能移动双腿。任何会切断其中一个头的能力（例如锋利武器特性）都不会导致玛古克死亡，但会使她失去被切断头的回合、动作和反应。针对单个生物的精神效果仅影响玛古克的一个头。

Regeneration 20 (Deactivated by Electric.name: 再生 20 (受到火焰或电击后失效) Regeneration 20 (Deactivated by Electricity or Fire)

Head Regrowth.name: 头部再生 Head Regrowth
Head Regrowth.description: 玛古克的再生能力能够使被砍下的头颅重新长出。在通过再生恢复生命值后，玛古克需要进行一次 @Check[flat|dc:10]检定。检定成功的话，缺失的头颅将完全恢复。如果玛古克失去最后一颗头颅，她会立即死亡。

Partial Armor.name: 部分着甲 Partial Armor
Partial Armor.description: 玛古克的护甲主要保护她的腹部和腿部，她的肩部和头部暴露在外。使用远程武器或触及武器攻击玛古克时，攻击检定获得+1 环境加值。在主持人的裁决下，其他能够轻易触及她头部高度的攻击（例如从高处发动的攻击）也获得此加值。

Sweeping Hammer.name: 战锤横扫 Sweeping Hammer
Sweeping Hammer.description:
需求 玛古克正持用战锤;

效果 玛古克对 @Template[type:cone|distance:15]的每个生物进行一次战锤打击。

Tenderized Feast.name: 松肉盛宴 Tenderized Feast
Tenderized Feast.description:
触发 玛古克使用战锤打击成功命中一个生物

效果 玛古克的一个头颅趁敌方晕头转向之际，对其发动一次颚咬打击，但攻击检定受到-2减值。

Graveknight (Umbra).name: 死墓骑士(暗影) Graveknight (Umbra)
Graveknight (Umbra).publicNotes: 死墓骑士是由被诅咒的铠甲赋予不死身的亡灵战士。

Rejuvenation.name: 复生 Rejuvenation
Rejuvenation.description:
当死墓骑士被摧毁时，其护甲会在为期 [[/gmr 1d10]] 的过程中重塑身体——若护甲被活着的宿主穿戴则会更加迅速。若身体在此之前被破坏，重塑过程将重新开始。

只有摧毁护甲(比如使用 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.r7ihOgKv19eJQnik]), 把死墓骑士传送至创生熔炉、或者丢进火山之中才能够永久摧毁死墓骑士。

Sacrilegious Aura.name: 亵渎灵光 Sacrilegious Aura
Sacrilegious Aura.description:
30 尺。

当有生物在灵光射程内使用命能法术或能力时，死墓骑士会自动尝试反制它，使用 [[/r 1d20+17 #Counteract]]{+17}反制调整值。

Devastating Blast.name: 毁灭爆震 Devastating Blast
Devastating Blast.description:
死墓骑士释放出 @Template[cone|distance:30] 的能量。区域内的生物承受 @Damage[11d6[cold]|options:area-damage](@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect])。

死墓骑士每 [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Devastating Blast]] 轮只能使用该能力一次。

Graveknight's Curse.name: 死墓骑士诅咒 Graveknight's Curse
Graveknight's Curse.description:
诅咒会影响任何穿戴死墓骑士护甲超过1小时以上的生物

豁免 @Check[will|dc:33] 豁免

潜伏期 1 小时

阶段 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{毁灭 Doomed 1} 且无法脱下护甲（1日）

阶段 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{毁灭 Doomed 2}, 速度承受-10尺状态减值，且无法脱下护甲（1日）

阶段 3 死亡并转化为护甲的死墓骑士。

Phantom Mount.name: 魅影坐骑 Phantom Mount
Phantom Mount.description:
HP 58; AC 27, 强韧 +17, 反射 +15, 意志 +14

死墓骑士召唤出一匹超自然坐骑，就如同升阶 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.WPKJOhEihhcIm2uQ] ，它升阶至的法术环级等于死墓骑士等级的一半。与异影驹不同的是，坐骑的AC和豁免检定加值均比死墓骑士的数值低4点，坐骑的生命值为死墓骑士生命值的三分之一（向下取整）。

若坐骑被摧毁，死墓骑士必须等待1小时才能再次使用该能力。

Weapon Master.name: 武器大师 Weapon Master
Weapon Master.description: 死墓骑士无论持用何种武器，均可以使用该武器的重击专精效果。

Disarming Rebuke.name: 责令卸武 Disarming Rebuke
Disarming Rebuke.description: 隆疱城狱卒试图对一个生物 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9] ，掷骰时有+2的环境加值。若成功，狱卒还能对目标进行一次闷棍打击。这两次攻击都计入其多重攻击减值，但减值仅在两次攻击都完成后才增加。

Punish Prisoners.name: 惩处囚犯 Punish Prisoners
Punish Prisoners.description: 隆疱城的狱卒能够迅速制服缺失装备的敌人，对未持武器或盾牌的生物进行所有攻击时额外造成1d6点精准伤害。即使这些生物使用了特殊的徒手格斗选项，比如颚咬、爪抓或特定姿态赋予的特殊攻击，此伤害依然适用。

Azorena.name: 阿佐瑞娜 Azorena
Azorena.allSaves: 对抗神术时 +1 状态加值

+1 Status Bonus Against Divine Spells.name: 对抗神术时+1状态加值 +1 Status Bonus Against Divine Spells

Whispered Secrets.name: 秘密耳语 Whispered Secrets
Whispered Secrets.description: 当阿佐瑞娜持有惧怖塑像时，她施展 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.c3b6LdLlQDPngNIb]时，会将誓灵的等级视为低2级。这尊雕像会作为她的次要施法者，总是为她的主要检定提供+4 的环境加值。

Drain Bonded Item.name: 汲取连结物品 Drain Bonded Item
Drain Bonded Item.description:
频率 每天一次

需求 阿佐瑞娜持有惧怖塑像

效果 在本回合内，阿佐瑞娜可以施放一个她今天已准备且已施放过的法术，无需消耗法术位。她仍需施展法术并满足法术的其他需求。

Able Rider.name: 老练骑手 Able Rider
Able Rider.description: 隆疱城勇士指挥坐骑，自动视为成功通过控制坐骑检定。然后他们可以 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]或拔出一把武器。

Spite-Screamer.name: 怨恨尖叫者 Spite-Screamer
Spite-Screamer.publicNotes:
尸妖是一类诞生自无法摆脱的怨恨循环的智慧不死生物。这种怨恨可能来自它们生前的歹意，也可能是由死灵魔法仪式人为灌输，这通常涉及亵渎墓葬地点。尸妖通常出没于埋骨地、地下墓穴或其他死者所在之地。它们饥渴的目标乃是生者——那些心脏尚在搏动、面色红润温暖的人会激起他们内心的仇恨。

尸妖的种类就和可能会成为它们的生者一样多。粗壮的莽汉、迅捷的潜行者和狡黠的修补匠都能成为弥补不同生态位的不同尸妖。环境也会影响尸妖的特殊能力与防御。例如，在世界上那些容易暴露于危险自然环境的地方，时常能找到冰霜尸妖（Frost wight），此外，因被遗弃于荒野而产生的怨怼也是一种常见的怨恨来源。自身的坚韧加上虚空能力的维持，令尸妖能在极端环境下存续，而不像低级不死生物那样腐烂。

一旦一只尸妖被迫从坟墓中苏醒，便会掀起一场浩劫。因为被尸妖的诅咒杀死的生物也会化作尸妖，所以只需要一个尸妖和几个倒霉的墓地访客，就能形成一场名副其实的尸潮。因此，精明的牧师和冒险者都知道，解决尸妖问题的最好办法就是迅速且彻底地消灭它们。不过，必须要注意，在摧毁亲代尸妖前一定要先消灭尸妖衍体，因为失去主人的衍体会获得创造属于它自己的衍体的能力。

Grave Weapon.name: 坟墓武器 Grave Weapon
Grave Weapon.description: 该尸妖被束缚在其死亡时拿着的弯刃大刀。

Final Spite.name: 最后的怨恨 Final Spite
Final Spite.description:
触发 尸妖的生命值（HP）被降至0

效果 尸妖被摧毁前进行一次打击。这次打击能施加怨恨侵蚀诅咒，但无法附加怨恨注能的效果。

Fueled by Spite.name: 怨恨注能 Fueled by Spite
Fueled by Spite.description: 每当一个生物由于尸妖施加的怨恨侵蚀诅咒而失去生命值，那个尸妖获得3点临时生命。

Cleaving Rush.name: 猛袭冲锋 Cleaving Rush
Cleaving Rush.description: 怨恨尖叫者行走，并在移动过程中的任意时刻进行两次近战打击。

Corrupting Spite.name: 怨恨侵蚀 Corrupting Spite
Corrupting Spite.description:
尸妖的无武装攻击与绑定武器的攻击会施加诅咒，令生物变得更加虚弱、充满怨念。若一个尸妖向一个已经受其影响的生物再次施加怨恨侵蚀，该生物必须进行一次新的检定，此次检定结果好于失败并不会如常产生效果，而是直接被忽略。

在受诅咒影响时死去的类人生物在 [[/gmr 1d4 #Wight Spawn Timer]]轮后复活为尸妖，新的尸妖不能使用怨恨侵蚀且处于@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{笨拙 Clumsy 2}，由杀死它的尸妖控制。如果它的创造者死了，或者存在了大约一个月后，新的尸妖就会获得自主并变成一个普通的尸妖。

豁免骰 @Check[fortitude|dc:22];

阶段 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{流失 Drained 1} (1 轮)

阶段2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{流失 Drained 2} 且无法将任何生物视作盟友 (1 轮)

阶段3 如同阶段2，但 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{流失 Drained 3} (1 轮)

阶段4 如同阶段2，但 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{流失 Drained 4} (1 轮)。

Battle Illusion.name: 战斗幻象 Battle Illusion
Battle Illusion.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:30] 或 @Check[religion|dc:30] （专家）两次来削弱作祟或 施展 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG] (4环；反制 DC 27) 来对抗幻象
Battle Illusion.hazarddescription: 干涸的灰烬之地景象消失，河流重新充盈，绿草蔓延大地。到处都能听到战斗的声音，所有盟友都不见了，取而代之的是来自独眼氏族的凶残兽人。
Battle Illusion.routine:
（1 动作； 幻术, 失能, 心灵, 异能）作祟持续施展幻术，让受害者感觉仿佛已被抛弃，且四面楚歌。作祟区域内的每个生物都必须进行一次 @Check[will|dc:28|traits:illusion,incapacitation,mental,occult] 豁免。

大成功 该生物不受影响，并在1分钟内暂时免疫该效果。

成功 该生物不受影响。

失败 该生物陷入 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ] 1轮。

大失败 该生物对独眼兽人幻象怀有敌意，必须将所有动作都用于向独眼兽人移动并攻击他们。这种敌意会持续1分钟，直到作祟被解除，或者直到其 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]。他们无法主动停止攻击他们认为是敌人的人。
Battle Illusion.reset: 作祟在所有生物离开背影地1 分钟后失效，但会立即重置。如果祖灵风暴未被平息，被解除的战斗幻象会在1 天后重置。

Cries of War.name: 战争哭嚎 Cries of War
Cries of War.description:
触发 两个或更多生物进入了作祟区域

效果 每个生物都会看到附近盟友的身影扭曲消散，被敌对的独眼兽人所取代。该区域内的每个生物必须进行一次 @Check[will|dc:30] 豁免。随后作祟进行先攻检定。

大成功 该生物识破了幻象，并在1 分钟内暂时免疫该作祟的流程。

成功 该生物不受影响。

失败 该生物相信幻象是真实的；如果他因作祟的流程陷入 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ] ，将无法在受到伤害时尝试无条件的检定来结束困惑状态。

大失败 如同失败，同时该生物会充满被抛弃和背叛的感觉。当该生物对作祟的流程效果进行掷骰时，必须掷两次骰子，并取较差的结果。这是一种厄运效果。

Great Ironbill.name: 大铁喙鸟 Great Ironbill
Great Ironbill.publicNotes:
大铁喙鸟是一种原产于泥海沿岸的类似鹳的生物，它以用其令人畏惧的喙发出极其响亮且令人不安的声音而闻名。

贝尔克赞的巨型动物饲养业是兽人引以为傲的产业，许多物种被培育用于日常生活。在战斗中，它们能为兽人提供对抗敌人的优势。

活体警报器

大铁喙鸟往往一动不动，显得异常诡异，因此当它们站在树丛或阴影中时很难被发现。再加上它们震耳欲聋的叫声，训练有素的铁喙鸟是出色的守卫。

Clatter.name: 闭合声响 Clatter
Clatter.description: 铁喙鸟会将喙合拢发出声响。 30 尺范围内的生物会受到 @Damage[2d10[sonic]] 伤害 （@Check[will|dc:22|basic] 豁免）。豁免失败的生物还会受到@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 1} （大失败时则受到 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 2} ）。

Flyby Attack.name: 飞掠攻击 Flyby Attack
Flyby Attack.description: 铁喙鸟飞行一次，并可在中途任意一点进行一次利爪打击。

Standstill.name: 停滞 Standstill
Standstill.description: 巨铁喙鸟令人不安地静止不动。铁喙鸟会使其下次打击的生物陷入 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]。

Palpares.name: 蚁狮 Palpares
Palpares.publicNotes:
古代矮人对泥海不屑一顾，认为淤泥中没什么值得开采的东西。兽人要勇敢得多，但即便如此，他们也对在淤泥中游弋的怪物心存忌惮。对于外人来说，蚁狮或许只是个用来告诫孩子别陷进流沙的警示故事，而泥海沿岸那些大坑也不过是雨季时出现的地质现象。然而，那些误入这些陷阱的人深知其中的危险。

很少有人了解蚁狮这种体型巨大的昆虫，它那厚实的腹部占据了其体型的大部分。它们似乎是独居的伏击猎手，在旱季冬眠，然后在洪水季节苏醒，挖出巨大的坑洞陷阱。这些陷阱能捕获各种体型的猎物，甚至偶尔会捕获迁徙的牛群，它们就埋伏在坑洞中央等待。虽然通常非常有耐心，但一旦受到惊扰，蚁狮能在其栖息的沙质土壤中迅速移动，用铲状的头部和锋利的钳子迎接闯入其领地的入侵者。

生命阶段

研究表明，蚁狮尚未进化到最终形态，因为在海岸边的巨大洞穴中发现了几丁质的痕迹以及部分变形的腐烂残骸。尽管没人见过成年的蚁狮并能活着回来讲述，但有传言称其完全成形的昆虫形态酷似洛瓦古格。人们一旦发现沙土中那些巨大的、裹着沙壳的蛹状物，便会迅速将其处理掉。有些城镇会举办大型烧烤活动，来收获成吨的“泥培根”。

Dig In.name: 开工 Dig In
Dig In.description:
触发 蚁狮在地面上时受到伤害

效果 蚁狮以最大速度向地下钻洞，扬起一层泥土形成的可减少11 点伤害的护盾。

Breaching Ambush.name: 潜伏突袭 Breaching Ambush
Breaching Ambush.description:
需求 蚁狮身处地下

效果 蚁狮以两倍于其速度的速度在生物下方挖掘地道，然后用钳子向上打击，打击结束后重新出现在地面。

Palpares Venom.name: 蚁狮毒液 Palpares Venom
Palpares Venom.description:
豁免 @Check[fortitude|dc:30]

最大持续时间 6 轮

阶段 1 @Damage[3d6[poison]] 伤害并且 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1} (1 轮)

阶段 2 @Damage[3d8[poison]] 伤害并且 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 2} (1 轮)

阶段 3 @Damage[3d10[poison]] 伤害并且 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF] (1 轮)

Pit Trap.name: 坑洞陷阱 Pit Trap
Pit Trap.description:
蚁狮挖掘地道，在自身周围形成一个 @Template[emanation|distance:30]中心深10 尺的倾斜坑洞。该区域内的所有生物必须进行一次 @Check[reflex|dc:30]豁免。

大成功 生物因震颤而移动到坑边之外的一个方格。

成功 如同大成功，但该生物陷入 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{笨拙 Clumsy 1} 1轮。

失败 生物跌入10 尺深的坑中，被撞得 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]。

大失败 如同失败，该生物还陷入 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{震慑 Stunned 1}。

Venomous Bite.name: 毒液噬咬 Venomous Bite
Venomous Bite.description:
需求 蚁狮 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]了一个生物

效果 蚁狮用螯肢注入毒液，造成 @Damage[3d8[acid]] 伤害，并使该生物暴露于蚁狮毒液中。

Frightful Standard.name: 恐怖之旗 Frightful Standard
Frightful Standard.description:
30 尺。 @Check[will|dc:27|options:area-effect,inflicts:frightened]。

只要莫洛格骑在坐骑上且其旗帜完好无损，在他灵光范围内的敌人就会受到恐怖威压效果。

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FrightfulPresence]

Battle Cry.name: 战斗嚎叫 Battle Cry
Battle Cry.description:
触发 莫洛格进行先攻检定

效果 莫洛格对一名可见敌人 [[/act demoralize]] 。

Trampling Charge.name: 践踏冲锋 Trampling Charge
Trampling Charge.description: 莫洛格催促血翼向前冲锋，践踏沿途的敌人。他命令她以她的速度行走或飞行，穿过沿途体型不超过大型的敌人的空间。血翼对穿过其空间的每个生物造成相当于其爪抓近战打击的伤害，生物需进行@Check[reflex|dc:31|basic] 豁免。若豁免失败，该生物在莫洛格的下一个回合结束前处于@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]状态。在此次移动过程中，他只能对每个生物造成一次伤害。

Weapon Adept.name: 武器熟练 Weapon Adept
Weapon Adept.description: 莫洛格能够在他的大砍刀和复合短弓之间切换，然后用切换后的武器进行打击。

Wyvern's Swoop.name: 双足飞龙俯冲 Wyvern's Swoop
Wyvern's Swoop.description:
需求 莫洛格骑乘着他的双足飞龙

效果 莫洛格命令他的坐骑行走两次。在这两次移动中的任何时候，他都可以对触及范围内的一个敌人或其远程武器射程的第一个射程增量内的一个敌人进行一次打击，攻击掷骰获得+1环境加值。如果他开始移动时的位置比目标高，则此次打击额外造成一个伤害骰的伤害。

Wight Spell Sniper.name: 尸妖法术狙击手 Wight Spell Sniper
Wight Spell Sniper.publicNotes:
尸妖是一类诞生自无法摆脱的怨恨循环的智慧不死生物。这种怨恨可能来自它们生前的歹意，也可能是由死灵魔法仪式人为灌输，这通常涉及亵渎墓葬地点。尸妖通常出没于埋骨地、地下墓穴或其他死者所在之地。它们饥渴的目标乃是生者——那些心脏尚在搏动、面色红润温暖的人会激起他们内心的仇恨。

尸妖的种类就和可能会成为它们的生者一样多。粗壮的莽汉、迅捷的潜行者和狡黠的修补匠都能成为弥补不同生态位的不同尸妖。环境也会影响尸妖的特殊能力与防御。例如，在世界上那些容易暴露于危险自然环境的地方，时常能找到冰霜尸妖（Frost wight），此外，因被遗弃于荒野而产生的怨怼也是一种常见的怨恨来源。自身的坚韧加上虚空能力的维持，令尸妖能在极端环境下存续，而不像低级不死生物那样腐烂。

一旦一只尸妖被迫从坟墓中苏醒，便会掀起一场浩劫。因为被尸妖的诅咒杀死的生物也会化作尸妖，所以只需要一个尸妖和几个倒霉的墓地访客，就能形成一场名副其实的尸潮。因此，精明的牧师和冒险者都知道，解决尸妖问题的最好办法就是迅速且彻底地消灭它们。不过，必须要注意，在摧毁亲代尸妖前一定要先消灭尸妖衍体，因为失去主人的衍体会获得创造属于它自己的衍体的能力。

Grave Weapon.name: 坟墓武器 Grave Weapon
Grave Weapon.description: 这个尸妖绑定着一把与它一同埋葬的匕首。别的尸妖可以绑定别的武器。

Drain Life.name: 吸取生命 Drain Life
Drain Life.description:
当尸妖狙击手使用徒手攻击或远程武器对活物造成伤害时, 尸妖狙击手获得2d6 点临时生命值，且该生物必须成功通过一次 @Check[fortitude|dc:22|options:inflicts:drained] 豁免，否则将受到 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{流失 Drained 1}。尸妖狙击手的后续攻击若造成伤害，且目标豁免失败，则流失增加1点，最多流失 4。

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.7EBdAHY7y06Bk4un]{效果:吸取生命（尸妖法术狙击手）Effect: Drain Life (Wight Spell Sniper)}

Drain the Prey.name: 汲取猎物 Drain the Prey
Drain the Prey.description:
触发尸妖法术狙击手杀死一个活着的生物

Effect 尸妖选择该生物的一个特征（不包括稀有度或体型）。在尸妖再次使用此能力之前，它在察觉检定中 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ] 具有该特征的生物时获得+2 环境加值，在生存检定中@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.EA5vuSgJfiHH7plD]具有该特征的生物时也获得+2 环境加值。尸妖的法术和能力对具有相同特征的生物的豁免DC 增加1。

Verthok the Reaper.name: 收割者维索克 Verthok the Reaper

Blade Ally.name: 利刃盟友 Blade Ally
Blade Ally.description: 在她的手中，维索克的兽人斩首斧是一件畏怖武器，并且她获得了斧重击精通效果。

Destructive Vengeance.name: 毁灭复仇 Destructive Vengeance
Destructive Vengeance.description:
触发 一名15 尺内的敌人对维索克造成伤害

效果 维索克所受伤害增加 @Damage[1d6[untyped]]，她对触发此效果的敌人造成@Damage[3d6[spirit]] 或 @Damage[3d6[void]] 伤害。受到毁灭复仇伤害的敌人受到 @Damage[4[persistent,spirit]] 伤害。直到维索克的下一个回合结束，她对触发此效果的生物的打击造成的伤害增加 2 点（伤害类型为她所选）。

Bloody Assault.name: 血腥猛击 Bloody Assault
Bloody Assault.description: 维索克进行两次近战打击，每次打击选择不同的对手；她的多重攻击减值正常适用于这些攻击。如果两次攻击都命中并造成伤害，每个被击中的生物都会受到 @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] 伤害。

Greater Cruelty.name: 高等酷虐 Greater Cruelty
Greater Cruelty.description: 如果维索克的下一个动作是对一个活体生物施展腐化之触，且目标豁免失败，则目标在1分钟内力竭、笨拙或@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{呆滞 Stupefied 1} （大失败则@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{呆滞 Stupefied 2） 。

Mask's Glare.name: 假面睥睨 Mask's Glare
Mask's Glare.description: 每日一次，维索克可以使用她的恶魔面具施展一次3环的 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4koZzrnMXhhosn0D]， 豁免DC为 @Check[will|dc:29]。

Occult Weather.name: 超自然天气 Occult Weather
Occult Weather.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:28] (专家) 将驱动天气的魔力重新导入其中，或者@Check[religion|dc:30] (专家) 驱逐一些被困的灵魂并削弱风暴；在任何组合下通过总共三次成功检定才能解除这个作祟
Occult Weather.hazarddescription: 天空突然变得昏暗，乌云翻滚，狂风呼啸，死者的面容在云层中显现，他们的哭喊声随风传来。
Occult Weather.routine: （4 动作）超自然天气加剧，狂风呼啸，雷电交加。超自然天气用3 个动作对三个随机生物发动闪电打击，优先攻击兽人或过去一轮内试图解除它的生物，然后用“尖啸之风”作为其最后一个动作。
Occult Weather.reset: 作祟在所有生物离开背影地1 分钟后失效，但会立即重置。如果祖灵风暴未被平息，被解除的超自然天气会在1 天后重置。

Thunder of Cries.name: 哭嚎雷鸣 Thunder of Cries
Thunder of Cries.description:
触发 一个生物进入了作祟区域

效果 乌云密布，雷声轰鸣，其中夹杂着这片土地上死者们的哀号和尖叫。这些幽灵的尖叫声对区域内的所有生物造成 @Damage[(3d6+13)[sonic]] 伤害 （@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] 豁免）。 作祟随后进行先攻检定。

Wind of Screams.name: 尖啸之风 Wind of Screams
Wind of Screams.description: 超自然天气使该区域周围的风力增强，对 @Template[type:line|distance:60]内的所有生物造成冲击。线形内的每个生物都必须进行一次 @Check[fortitude|dc:32] 豁免，否则将被 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]，并受到 @Damage[(1d10+6)[bludgeoning]] 伤害。

Champion of Zagresh.name: 扎格雷什神卫 Champion of Zagresh

Destructive Vengeance.name: 毁灭复仇 Destructive Vengeance
Destructive Vengeance.description:
触发 扎格雷什神卫受到15尺范围内敌人的伤害

效果 扎格瑞什的神卫所受伤害增加 @Damage[2d6[untyped]] 并对触发此效果的敌人造成@Damage[2d6[void]] 伤害。此外，在神卫下一个回合结束前，该神卫对触发此效果敌人的攻击额外造成2 点虚能伤害。

Fit of Rage.name: 释放狂怒 Fit of Rage
Fit of Rage.description:
频率 每天一次

效果神卫挥动他的巨棒作大范围挥击，对触及范围内的所有生物进行一次近战攻击。若造成大成功，则敌人会被向后击退5尺。这是一次强迫移动。每次攻击都会计入神卫的多重攻击减值，但直到他完成所有攻击为止，减值不会增加。

Intimidating Strike.name: 惧怖打击 Intimidating Strike
Intimidating Strike.description: 扎格雷什神卫进行一次近战打击。若命中并造成伤害，目标受到[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 1}，若触发重击则目标受到@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 2}。

Floodslain Orc.name: 洪溺兽人 Floodslain Orc
Floodslain Orc.publicNotes:
多年来，无数的兽人成为了洪淹的牺牲品。有的猝不及防被洪水淹没，有的在试图拯救亲人、贵重物品、牲畜或财产时丧生，还有一些在与其他兽人的冲突中被卷入了汹涌的洪流。

每年，洪淹的突然来袭都会在贝尔克赞夺走许多生灵的生命。这些在洪水中丧生的生物在生命的最后时刻所感受到的惊恐与绝望有时足以让他们死而复生，成为“洪溺生物”。随着夺走他们生命的洪水退去，这些不死生物便开始寻找新的同伴加入它们的行列。虽然它们的身体支离破碎，但那些生前认识它们的人往往还能认出它们。

在贝尔克赞之外，洪溺生物极为罕见，这使得一些学者猜测洪淹的水域可能被某种诅咒所笼罩。也有人认为，位于绞首塔的附近可能是原因所在。如果真是这样，那么随着默语暴君从那座监狱中逃脱并继续其阴谋，死坟末土很快也会出现被洪水淹死的生物。

惊恐的眼睛

所有洪溺生物都有一个显著特征，那就是他们睁得大大的、充满惊恐的眼睛。洪溺生物的目光永远定格在临死前那惊恐万分的表情上。据说有些被洪溺生物仅用一个眼神就能将这种恐惧传递给他人。

搜寻水源

洪溺生物往往会向低洼处移动，本能地寻找水源。贝尔克赞平坦的泛滥平原意味着这些尸体的移动方向难以预测。老兽人工匠中有一句俗语：“建在高处，保持干燥”，但很多人会补充一句：“建在低处，死尸漂流”。

Drowning Touch.name: 溺水之触 Drowning Touch
Drowning Touch.description:
当洪溺生物以无武装近战攻击伤害一个生物时，目标的肺部开始充满水，尝试一次@Check[type:fortitude|against:will] 豁免对抗洪溺生物的意志DC。受到溺水之触影响的目标可以花费一个动作咳嗽以尝试恢复，会立即让他们对效果进行新一次的强韧豁免。成功会使之前的判定结果提高 1 级。大成功会使之前的判定结果提高 2 级，而大失败则会使其降低 1 级。

大成功 该生物不受影响。

成功 该生物 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{缓慢 Slowed 1} 持续1轮。

失败 该生物 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{缓慢 Slowed 2} 持续1轮。

大失败 该生物缓慢2，并处于溺水状态。

Floodslain Spawn.name: 溺亡衍体 Floodslain Spawn
Floodslain Spawn.description: 当一个生物或类人生物被洪溺生物杀死，其尸体若在水中浸泡 24 小时，则会复活为洪溺生物。新复活的洪溺生物不会受杀死它的洪溺生物的控制。

Sodden Ground.name: 浸湿地面 Sodden Ground
Sodden Ground.description: 20 尺。洪溺生物周围不断有水流淌，使其周围的地面变得湿滑。光环内的地面对于所有非洪溺生物的生物而言都是困难地形。

Vomit Flotsam.name: 呕吐碎流 Vomit Flotsam
Vomit Flotsam.description: 洪溺生物体内充满了其惨死时的残骸，它们能将其吐向敌人。洪溺生物向@Template[type:cone|distance:15]呕吐。该区域内的任何生物会受到@Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] 伤害（ @Check[type:reflex|against:fortitude|basic|options:area-effect] 豁免对抗洪溺生物的强韧 DC）。

Trampling Livestock.name: 牲畜践踏 Trampling Livestock
Trampling Livestock.disable: @Check[religion|dc:28] 来驱除动物的精魂, 或 @Check[nature|dc:26] 或 @Check[stabling-lore|dc:24] 来安抚它们
Trampling Livestock.hazarddescription: 几头幽灵猛犸象从地板下冒了出来，惊恐地用蹄子刨着地面，随后朝着门口狂奔而去。

Sudden Stampede.name: 突然奔逃 Sudden Stampede
Sudden Stampede.description:
触发 兽栏的门打开

Effect 猛犸象的灵魂惊慌失措，化为实体形态，朝着门口狂奔以求逃脱。所有位于门边20 尺范围内的生物承受 @Damage[(4d10+26)[force]] 伤害(@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] 豁免)。

Skeletal Drummer.name: 骷髅鼓手 Skeletal Drummer
Skeletal Drummer.publicNotes: 对一些人来说，音乐远不止是一种爱好，更是让他们走火入魔的迷魂汤。每个为了音乐而生也为之而死的音乐家，都有可能以拨弦亡骨的形态归来，尤其是那些在完成自己的巨作或获得自认为应得的赞誉之前就去世的音乐家。哪怕身为不死之躯也竭力想要证明自己，拨弦亡骨经常向知名演奏家挑起音乐决斗。
Skeletal Drummer.blurb: 变体拨弦亡骨 Variant fiddling bones

Rhapsodic Flourish.name: 华丽狂想曲 Rhapsodic Flourish
Rhapsodic Flourish.description:
触发 骷髅鼓手需要对一个听觉或音波效果进行一次豁免检定

效果 骷髅鼓手演奏一段副歌，能抵消声波振动。他以一次 @Check[performance] 检定代替触发效果的豁免检定。如果表演检定成功，声波反馈会反弹至触发效果的来源，对其造成 @Damage[1d6[sonic]] 伤害；若失败，则对骷髅鼓手造成 @Damage[2d6[sonic]] 点音波伤害。

Destructive Finale.name: 毁灭终曲 Destructive Finale
Destructive Finale.description:
需求 向战进军能力正在生效

效果 骷髅鼓手敲响喧闹的终曲。它选择 @Template[type:cone|distance:30] 或 @Template[type:emanation|distance:15], 范围，对该区域内的所有生物造成 @Damage[4d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] 伤害（@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] 豁免)。 向战进军立即结束，并且骷髅鼓手在[[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Destructive Finale]] 回合内不能使用向战进军或毁灭终曲能力。

March to War.name: 向战进军 March to War
March to War.description:
骷髅鼓手手敲奏出有节奏的鼓点，迫使他人随之行进。 @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] 范围内的所有生物必须进行一次 @Check[will|dc:19] 豁免，骷髅鼓手对此免疫。该效果持续到骷髅鼓手的下一个回合结束，但骷髅鼓手可以在所有受影响且滞留在弥漫范围内的生物上维持该效果。

大成功 此生物不受影响且临时免疫此效果1小时。

成功 该生物无法使用具有移动特征的动作，除非是用于行军。行军在此处意味着该生物使用行走动作移动至多半速。如果该生物有其他速度，他也可以使用这些移动方式来行军。

失败 如同成功，且生物每轮必须使用至少一个动作来行军。

Bull Stampede.name: 公牛奔踏 Bull Stampede
Bull Stampede.disable: 4次 @Check[athletics|dc:27], @Check[intimidation|dc:27], @Check[nature|dc:27] （专家）, 或 @Check[survival|dc:27] (专家) 检定以引导牛群、为其在货栈区找到路径或使其保持秩序。
Bull Stampede.hazarddescription: 狂躁的野牛群在街道上横冲直撞。
Bull Stampede.routine:
(1 动作) 牛群向前推进，将沿途的一切都踩踏在脚下。每个试图控制牛群的生物必须进行一次 @Check[reflex|dc:30] 豁免，否则就会被卷入牛群的踩踏，受到@Damage[(2d12+15)[bludgeoning]] 伤害；本轮成功进行技能检定以解除此危害的生物在反射豁免上有+1 的环境加值。

大成功 该生物不受伤害。

成功 该生物受到的伤害减半。

失败 该生物受到全额伤害。

大失败 该生物受到双倍伤害，并且下一轮无法尝试解除危害，因为他们必须花时间从踩踏中脱身。
Bull Stampede.stealthdetails: (受训) 以预测兽群的动向

Rampage.name: 狂暴 Rampage
Rampage.description:
触发 一个生物将公牛从牛棚中放出

效果 公牛群开始狂奔，危害进行先攻检定。

Orc Rampager.name: 兽人狂怒者 Orc Rampager
Orc Rampager.publicNotes:
在战斗中因杀戮欲望而狂暴的兽人狂怒者是令人畏惧的战士。他们常常作为兽人氏族的精锐突击部队。他们训练自己在敌阵中快速穿梭，四面出击，以扰乱敌人的防御。一群协同作战的兽人狂怒者的速度和狂暴令人胆寒。

贝尔克赞的兽人骨子里就是生存者。在严酷环境的磨砺下，他们精于资源管理，善于在匮乏中求生存。除了春末夏初洪水盟约休战期的相对和平，贝尔克赞的兽人氏族几乎总是相互争斗，不过这些冲突很少升级为战争。取而代之的是为获取物资而进行的突袭、为划定边界或夺回失地而发生的冲突，以及因个人恩怨而引发的争斗。在贝尔克赞旅行的外来者常常卷入这些纷争之中，有时甚至会成为冲突的导火索。

一个特定氏族的成员通常分布得很散，而非集中在一起，所以交流和协调是断断续续的。就连乌尔吉尔的空手氏族兽人也很少整年都待在城里。至少，当野牛到来时，他们会前往洪淹大道几英里之外的地方，而当兴致来了，他们甚至会走得更远。

兽人信仰

兽人诸神体系遭受如此多突如其来的冲击，信徒们发现自己正处于一个令人困惑的变革时期。兰尼什拉、塞泽里安和扎格雷什的狂热信徒们对取代他们旧神的新神并不买账。其中一些狂热分子仍紧紧依附于旧信仰，而且许多人仍保有其旧神的一丝力量，这更增添了混乱。

回收利用

兽人善于将材料重新利用。贝尔克赞没有肥沃的土壤，也没有丰富的贵重金属和矿产资源。因此，兽人的工匠们依靠从战斗中获取的战利品或与外界的贸易来获取原材料。一把普通的刀具或一口煮锅，即便年代久远，仍能保持良好的使用状态也是很常见的。即便工具损坏了，兽人的工匠们也会想方设法尽可能多地加以利用。

Rampaging Strikes.name: 狂怒打击 Rampaging Strikes
Rampaging Strikes.description: 兽人狂怒者进行一次移动，并在移动过程中的任意时刻进行两次近战攻击。每次攻击的目标必须是不同的生物。这些攻击计入狂怒者的多重攻击减值，但减值不会增加，直到狂怒打击完成之后。

Tyrant's Orders.name: 暴君的命令 Tyrant's Orders
Tyrant's Orders.disable: @Check[deception|dc:28] （专家）或@Check[performance|dc:28] （专家） 来装作加入了战斗, 或 @Check[religion|dc:30] （大师） 驱离亚格雷卡什; 成功通过三次任意组合的技能才能解除作祟。
Tyrant's Orders.hazarddescription: 亚格雷卡什似乎在斥责那些不思进取的士兵，指责他们没有尽到自己的职责。
Tyrant's Orders.reset: 当所有生物离开白骨堡垒的区域1 分钟后，作祟就会失效，但会立即重置。如果祖灵风暴未被平息，暴君的命令在被禁用1 天后会重置。

Command of the Legion.name: 军团之令 Command of the Legion
Command of the Legion.description:
触发玩家角色在穿越祖灵风暴的技能检定中大失败

效果 亚格雷卡什向玩家角色们下达命令，让他们加入战斗。所有玩家角色必须进行一次 @Check[will|dc:27] 豁免；兽人和半兽人玩家角色-1减值 。

大成功 该生物不受影响。

成功 该生物获得@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{惊惧 Frightened 1}，持续1分钟。

失败 该生物被迫加入暗影军团作战，承受 @Damage[(2d12+13)[mental]] 伤害。

大失败 如同失败，但伤害加倍。

Old Herok.name: 老赫罗克 Old Herok
Old Herok.publicNotes:
名为湖怪的传说生物潜伏于偏远的湖泊中，他们总是能以某种方式成为湖畔社区的酒馆传说。对一些旅行者来说，每座规模较大的湖泊似乎都会被城镇环绕，且镇上到处都是声称目击过湖怪的渔民。这些难以琢磨的生物主要栖息在湖泊中凉爽幽暗的地区。有些人声称湖怪是海蛇和蛇龙的某种分支，但在人们已经发现的这些生物之间并未找到足够让人信服的联系。

湖怪具有许多海蛇不具备的特征，比如能够理解简单的语言。他们避免与外界产生接触且时刻隐藏自身的本性有时会与同样强烈的好奇心产生冲突，想要窥视湖畔的人们。当湖怪没能忍耐住，想要浮上湖面窥探位于湖岸边或者漂浮在水面上的不同寻常的某人（或某物）时，人们就会目击到湖怪。

这些生物的寿命极长，可以活数十年或者甚至数个世纪，而且食量很小。这使得湖怪可以在不浮上水面的前提下在湖泊中存活多年，即便身处缺乏充足食物来源的淡水水体中也是如此。湖怪可能会在湖床的淤泥中躺上数年，湖怪那难以捉摸的秉性只会让他们那传说中的名声变得更大。当有宠物或儿童在湖泊附近失踪时，就会有传闻说是被本地湖怪抓走了，进而产生民间传说警告居民们不要独自在湖水旁边探险。

大多数湖怪被描绘为蛇形或者颈部很长的爬行动物，但也有一部分看起来像是拉长了的怪异海豹或鲸鱼、大得不可思议的海马、或者颈部很长且生长着类似板龙（elasmosauruses）的桨状器官。
Old Herok.blurb: 变体湖怪 Variant water orm

Slow Metabolism.name: 代谢缓慢 Slow Metabolism
Slow Metabolism.description: 湖怪可以在不进食的情况下存活10年。超出此限制后，湖怪的饥饿就会让他陷入 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{缓慢 Slowed 1}，但这并不会影响他的寿命。由于饥饿而陷入缓慢的湖怪可以通过使用活吞进食来移除该状态。

Undetectable.name: 难以侦测 Undetectable
Undetectable.description: 湖怪会自动反制任何对他进行的侦测（detection）、揭示（revelation）或探知（scrying）的预言尝试，使用他的隐秘调整值来进行反制检定。

Sand Skim.name: 沙地滑行 Sand Skim
Sand Skim.description: 老赫罗克能够化为一团盘绕的沙子，在流沙表面下潜行。处于这种形态时，他在流沙中游泳的运动检定自动成功，并且隐秘检定获得+4的环境加值。如果生物进入他的空间，他可以立即恢复原状并以反应动作尝试擒获该生物。老赫罗克能保持这种形态8小时，可使用具有专注特征的单动作来恢复成正常的形态。

Ankhrav Hive Mother.name: 掘地虫巢母 Ankhrav Hive Mother
Ankhrav Hive Mother.publicNotes:
掘地虫巢母是一种可怕的掠食者，人们可以很容易地将其与普通的掘地虫区分开来，不仅因为它们更大的体型，还因为它们有一对锋利的、象螳螂一样的巨大前肢。

掘地虫是巨大的、穴居的节肢类捕食者，在乡村地区常被当地居民当做一种常见的天灾。

Tremorsense (Imprecise) 90 feet.name: 震颤感知（模糊感官）90 尺 Tremorsense (Imprecise) 90 feet

Armor-Rending Bite.name: 裂甲噬咬 Armor-Rending Bite
Armor-Rending Bite.description: 巢母进行1次虫颚打击，一旦打击命中，可以对目标的护甲造成酸蚀伤害，并且该酸蚀伤害无视目标护甲的硬度。

Frenzy Pheromone.name: 狂暴信息素 Frenzy Pheromone
Frenzy Pheromone.description:
巢母释放一种信息素，使得 @Template[emanation|distance:100] 范围内其他掘地虫获得 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{迅捷 Quickened 1}，持续到巢母下轮行动开始为止。该额外动作仅仅可以用来掘进、移动、或打击。

巢母在 [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Frenzy Pheromone]]轮内无法再次释放信息素。

Spray Acid.name: 喷射酸液 Spray Acid
Spray Acid.description:
巢母对 @Template[cone|distance:60]范围喷射酸蚀，造成 @Damage[8d6[acid],1d6[persistent,acid]|options:area-damage]{8d6酸蚀伤害和1d6持续酸蚀伤害 8d6 acid damage and 1d6 persistent acid damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect] 豁免)。

巢母在 [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Spray Acid]]轮内无法再次喷射酸液。

Magical Forge.name: 魔法熔炉 Magical Forge
Magical Forge.publicNotes:
This huge forge or oven is animated to protect a workspace or kitchen, travel alongside its owner, or both.

Many animated objects have useful functions but become dangers when uncontrolled.

Construct Armor (Hardness 8).name: Construct Armor (Hardness 8)
Construct Armor (Hardness 8).description: Like normal objects, an animated furnace has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage the furnace takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once an animated furnace is reduced to fewer than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks, removing the Hardness and reducing its Armor Class to 24.

Fan the Flames.name: Fan the Flames
Fan the Flames.description: The animated furnace opens its door and fans its flames in a @Template[cone|distance:30] that deals @Damage[5d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic|options:area-effect] save).

War Pig.name: War Pig
War Pig.publicNotes:
While not native to Belkzen, these swine instead descended from livestock raided and re-homed millennia ago during orc raids. Generations of selective breeding favored larger animals suited to Belkzen's harsh environment and fearsome predators, resulting in the modern war pig. During the flood season, these pigs gorge and grow fat, gradually consuming their reserves to become leaner and meaner during the rest of the year.

Arguably domesticated, war pigs rarely remain in captivity for more than a few generations. Most orcs loathe the notion of their swine becoming too tame, instead releasing part of their herd every year to grow fierce before recapturing them seasons later. The downside is that these feral swine associate humanoids with food. Rogue war pigs sometimes form roving gangs that uproot fields, run amok, and even knock over trade caravans to eat rations (and the occasional merchant).

The "war" in war pigs comes from their use as mounts. With the right motivation, a war pig is a porcine juggernaut able to carry a fully armored orc into combat. Without discipline, care, and treats, that same pig can become 600 pounds of willful chaos that's as likely to trample foes as it is to flee combat, find a mud puddle, and crush its rider after flopping down to vigorously wallow.

Squealed Outrage.name: Squealed Outrage
Squealed Outrage.description:
Trigger The war pig is damaged by an adjacent creature

Effect The war pig makes a hoof Strike at the triggering creature.

Bulldoze.name: Bulldoze
Bulldoze.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The war pig Shoves an adjacent creature, after which it can either Stride or Step.

Wallow.name: Wallow
Wallow.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The war pig rapidly rolls over. If it has a rider, the war pig @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.nZMQh4AaBr291TUf]{Bucks} as a free action. The war pig gains 1d8 temporary Hit Points for 1 minute and can then attempt an immediate flat check to recover from a single source of persistent acid, fire, or mental damage. If the war pig Wallows in an area with ample mud, water, loose soil, or similar material, the war pig instead gains 2d8 temporary Hit Points, for 1 minute and reduces the flat check DC to recover from persistent damage by 1.

Ardax's Puzzle Box.name: Ardax's Puzzle Box
Ardax's Puzzle Box.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:19] (trained), @Check[thievery|dc:18], and @Check[perception|dc:18] simultaneously by at least 3 characters; one of these checks can be replaced by a Strike against AC 19, and one can be replaced by targeting the box with a spell that has the electricity trait and deals at least 1 damage (this ignores the box's Hardness).
Ardax's Puzzle Box.hazarddescription: A chest-sized puzzle box is covered in intricate designs, with no immediately obvious way to open it.

Shocking Failure.name: Shocking Failure
Shocking Failure.description:
Trigger A creature fails one of the disable checks

Effect An electric jolt shocks all creatures who were attempting to open the box. This deals @Damage[(2d8+5)[electricity]]{2d8+5 nonlethal electricity} damage, and each creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save.

Yagrekash.name: Yagrekash
Yagrekash.publicNotes:
Einherjar are mighty warriors chosen by valkyries from the ranks of those slain in terrible and legendary battles. Forged from the souls of the greatest warriors, the implacable einherjar serve as the foot soldiers of pantheons, skilled in handto-hand combat and slaying giants.

Einherjar often come from warrior cultures, including Ulfen vikings (like the einherji represented in this entry), particularly fierce pirates from the Shackles, and even Osirian conquerors. They can be chosen from wherever war and might hold sway; many deities who hold the call of battle and the pursuit of physical power more sacred than concepts of good and evil might count einherjar warriors and valkyrie choosers of the slain among their chosen servitors. For example, Gorum, Besmara, and Sekhmet have all elevated fallen worshippers as einherjar. Einherjar dedicated to different deities often wield weapons or possess varying cosmetic appearances based on their deity's preferred weapon and their place of death; however, they're universally stalwart, implacable, and efficiently deadly. Einherjar with two-handed weapons or who arise from other backgrounds often have different abilities in place of Jotun Slayer and Instant Repair.

Challenge Foe.name: Challenge Foe
Challenge Foe.description: Yagrekash challenges one creature she can see to single combat, attempting to [[/act demoralize]] that target. This target remains her foe until it's defeated, it flees, or the encounter ends. Yagrekash gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to her number of weapon damage dice against her designated foe but takes an equivalent circumstance penalty to damage against any other creature.

Mask of Pain.name: Mask of Pain
Mask of Pain.description:
Trigger Yagrekash succeeds at a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2u915NdUyQan6uKF]{Demoralize} check

Effect The creature Demoralized takes @Damage[4d6[mental]] damage and is then temporarily immune to additional damage from Mask of Pain for 24 hours.

Warmask of Power.name: Warmask of Power
Warmask of Power.description: Yagrekash's echo form retains her painted divine warmask dedicated to Zagresh, allowing her to cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Gb7SeieEvd0pL2Eh]{Sure Strike} as an innate divine spell once per day.

Warcat of Rull.name: Warcat of Rull
Warcat of Rull.publicNotes:
These fierce hippo-sized cats with scaly, armored skin can only be bred and trained by the most skilled handlers.

Belkzen's megafauna husbandry is a source of orc pride, with many species bred for everyday life. In battle, they provide an edge against foes.

Apex Predator

The warcat of Rull is a stealthy predator that roams a large territory, hunting alone or with a mate. Even when nursing cubs, warcats hunt for themselves and teach their cubs to be self-sufficient. Warcats fear no challenge, preying on megafauna, giants, and even young dragons.

Piercing Fangs.name: Piercing Fangs
Piercing Fangs.description:
Requirements The warcat has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}

Effect The warcat clamps on to the creature's neck. The creature takes @Damage[(3d12+10)[piercing]] damage plus @Damage[1d4[bleed]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:33|basic] save).

Pounce.name: Pounce
Pounce.description: The warcat of Rull Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the warcat began this action @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}, it remains hidden until after the attack.

Cursebound.name: Cursebound
Cursebound.description: Using a cursebound ability makes Ungukk @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} or increases his enfeebled value by 1, to a maximum of 3. If he reaches @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 3}, he also becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

Curse of the Fallen Divine.name: Curse of the Fallen Divine
Curse of the Fallen Divine.description: Ungukk increases the damage of a spell he casts by 1 die.

Quickened Casting.name: Quickened Casting
Quickened Casting.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect If Ungukk's next action is to cast a cantrip or a spell of 4th rank or lower, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum of 1 action).

Roar of Zagresh.name: Roar of Zagresh
Roar of Zagresh.description: With a mighty roar, Ungukk unleashes void energy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30]. Creatures in the area must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:30|options:area-effect,damaging-effect,inflicts:doomed] save or take @Damage[6d6[void]|options:area-damage] damage and become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 2} on a critical failure).

Undying Conviction.name: Undying Conviction
Undying Conviction.description: 30 feet. Ungukk's undead allies within his aura gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws to resist vitality damage and to Will saving throws to resist effects that would make them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9qGBRpbX9NEwtAAr]{Controlled}.

Hard to Target.name: Hard to Target
Hard to Target.description: With their loose, shifting skin, a skinslough's vital organs are difficult to locate. Each time a skinslough would take precision damage or extra damage from a critical hit, it attempts a @Check[flat|dc:5]. On a success, the additional damage is negated.

Slough.name: Slough
Slough.description:
Trigger The skinslough gains a persistent damage condition or becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}

Effect The skinslough sheds part of its skin and attempts a @Check[flat|dc:5] to end the condition.

Skin Net.name: Skin Net
Skin Net.description:
The skinslough interacts to pull off part of its skin and throw it at a creature within 30 feet, making a ranged attack with a +12 modifier. If it hits, the target takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 round and must make a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save. If the skinslough critically hits, the target gets a result one degree of success worse than the outcome of its Fortitude save.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.2tOVDTjCZVx4uEjP]{Effect: Skin Net}

Critical Success As success, and the creature automatically succeeds at saving throws against the skinslough's Skin Net for 24 hours.

Success The target is unaffected except for the penalty to Speed.

Failure The target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Critical Failure The target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Sloughing Sickness.name: Sloughing Sickness
Sloughing Sickness.description:
This horrifying disease can make a target's skin slough away.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:18]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect (1 hour)

Stage 2 @Damage[(2d4+4)[void]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 @Damage[(4d4+4)[void]] damage, clumsy 1, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} (1 day).

Double Shot.name: Double Shot
Double Shot.description: The porphyry guard makes two longbow Strikes targeting two different creatures within the longbow's first range increment. Both Strikes uses the guard's current multiple attack penalty, but each strike takes a –2 penalty.

Weapon Master.name: Weapon Master
Weapon Master.description: The porphyry guard has access to the critical specialization effects of any weapons they wield.

Troll.name: Troll
Troll.publicNotes:
Forest trolls are gangly giants who stalk the fringes of civilization. They rely on their incredible strength to overpower foes with their vicious claws and toothy maws. These trolls stand anywhere from 12 to 16 feet tall, though they prefer to hunch for comfort and to lull foes into a false sense of security. Trolls are truly creatures of the forest, deeply linked in flesh and blood to the verdant habitats they consider their territory. In fact, when a forest troll is killed, their flesh turns into blackened lumps of charcoal, often still glowing with coals. Despite that, these creatures roam farther and wider than most of their kin, to the extent that they're the first creatures a common person thinks of when they hear the word "troll."

Slavering, cruel, invincible brutes: this is the villager's stock description for the dread monsters known as trolls. The roots of these stories are undoubtedly true. Trolls' flesh endlessly regrows, going so far as to sprout aberrant limbs or additional heads if not pruned, and a bottomless hunger is required to feed such unfettered growth. Even in the process of glutting themselves, however, trolls find opportunities to taunt their prey and inflict petty cruelties.

A troll's ability to survive is so strong that they believe even the smallest scrap of flesh will slowly regenerate into a new form, suffering as all the powers of the land are gathered to revive them. Despite the pain, trolls speak of this unassailable vitality as a blessing from their creator. Few trolls have heard the laughter of demons who claim that creator cursed the trolls and cast them down from lofty heights, binding them so they could never rise again.

Trolls prefer to remain solitary, keeping every scrap of food for themselves. In rare instances, an old and powerful troll comes to lead groups of trolls. Such warleaders possess enough cunning to lead their hordes in devastating raids and massacres, and their presence permanently alters the surrounding ecosystem. This link to their environment is an often misunderstood aspect of trollkind, and grows more acute with a troll's age and power. That's not to say trolls are valorous protectors of nature. They're vicious and territorial, and will blight their own territory forever if it means more to eat for a day.

Easily Misled.name: Easily Misled
Easily Misled.description: The forest troll gets a –4 circumstance penalty to their Perception DC against Deception checks.

Furious Flailing.name: Furious Flailing
Furious Flailing.description:
Trigger The forest troll takes electricity or fire damage

Effect The troll makes a claw Strike against a random creature within its reach. If the troll has persistent fire damage, they attempt a @Check[flat|dc:15] to remove it.

Chase Prey.name: Chase Prey
Chase Prey.description: The forest troll rushes forward on all fours, Striding and then making two claw Strikes.

Floodslain Mage.name: Floodslain Mage
Floodslain Mage.publicNotes:
The sudden, crashing waters of the Deluge kill many creatures in Belkzen each year. The surprise and terror these victims of the Deluge feel during their last moments is sometimes enough to raise them from the dead as floodslain. As the waters that killed them recede, the undead seek others to join them. Despite their broken, shattered bodies, floodslain are often recognizable to those who knew them in life.

Floodslain creatures are rare outside Belkzen, leading some scholars to speculate that the waters of the Deluge are somehow cursed. Others believe the proximity of Gallowspire could be the cause. If so, this means the Gravelands could soon see floodslain arise there too, as the Whispering Tyrant furthers his plots after escaping that prison.

Panicked Eyes

One distinctive feature all floodslain share are their wide, panic-stricken eyes. A floodslain's gaze is permanently frozen in the expression of terror the creature felt moments before its death. Some floodslain are rumored to have the ability to spread this fear to others with a mere glance.

In Search of Water

Floodslain tend to move toward lower ground, instinctively seeking water. Belkzen's flat floodplains, however, mean that the creatures wander in unpredictable ways. A common saying among old orc crafters is "build on high, keep dry," but many add "build low, the dead flow."

Drowning Touch.name: Drowning Touch
Drowning Touch.description:
The target's lungs begin to fill with water, and they must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save. A target affected by Drowning Touch can spend a single action coughing in an attempt to recover, which immediately lets them attempt a new Fortitude save against the effect. A success improves the previous result by 1 step. A critical success improves the previous result by 2, and a critical failure decreases it by 1.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is slowed 2 and drowning.

Floodslain Spawn.name: Floodslain Spawn
Floodslain Spawn.description: A living animal or humanoid killed by a floodslain creature will rise as a floodslain if their corpse is left in water for 24 hours. The new floodslain isn't under the control of the floodslain creature that killed it.

Sodden Ground.name: Sodden Ground
Sodden Ground.description: 20 feet. Water flows endlessly from a floodslain creature, making the area around it slippery. The ground in the aura is difficult terrain for all non-floodslain creatures.

Vomit Flotsam.name: Vomit Flotsam
Vomit Flotsam.description: The floodslain vomits flotsam in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15]. Any creature in the area takes @Damage[5d8[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic|options:area-effect] save).

Orc Doomsayer.name: Orc Doomsayer
Orc Doomsayer.publicNotes:
Driven by fervor bordering on zealotry, doomsayers spread the word of their destructive deities. Many are faithful followers of recently fallen gods. Though their deity has been cast down, an orc doomsayer's unwavering faith allows them to hold onto a sliver of their god's essence to power their abilities.

The orcs of Belkzen are survivors to the core. Shaped by the unforgiving landscape, they're experts at managing resources and making do with scarcity. Aside from the relative peace from late spring through early summer during the Flood Truce, Belkzen's orc holds are nearly always at odds with each other, though these conflicts rarely rise to the level of war. Instead, there are raids for supplies, skirmishes to establish borders or retake lost territory, and feuds over personal slights. Outsiders who travel in Belkzen often become embroiled in these conflicts; sometimes they even trigger the fights themselves.

The members of a given hold are often quite spread out, rather than centralized, so communication and coordination is sporadic. Even the Empty Hand orcs of Urgir rarely stay in the city all year long. At the very least, they travel a few miles to the Flood Road when the aurochs arrive, and they may wander even further afield when the mood strikes.

Orc Faith

With so many sudden disruptions to the orc pantheon, the faithful find themselves in a confusing time of change. Fervent worshippers of Lanishra, Sezelrian, and Zagresh don't particularly like the new deities who have usurped their old gods. Some of these zealots desperately cling to their old faiths, and many still hold a sliver of their fallen deity's power, further adding to the confusion.

Salvage

Orcs are experts at repurposing materials for their own use. Belkzen doesn't have rich soil or abundant deposits of valuable metals and minerals. Instead, orc crafters rely on salvage from battles or trade with outsiders for their raw materials. It's common for a basic tool, such as a knife or cooking pot, to be very old yet kept in perfect working condition. Even when a tool breaks, orc crafters find ways to reuse as much of it as they can manage.

Dooming Proclamation.name: Dooming Proclamation
Dooming Proclamation.description:
The orc doomsayer shouts pronouncements that presage the unstoppable approach of a cataclysmic event. Each enemy within 30 feet attempts a @Check[will|dc:21] save.

Critical Success No effect and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature's mind fills with images of the end times. The creature takes @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Infused Strikes.name: Infused Strikes
Infused Strikes.description: The orc doomsayer's melee Strikes deal an extra 1d8 void damage.

Biggest Fan!.name: Biggest Fan!
Biggest Fan!.description:
Trigger An ally Kar can see critically succeeds at a skill check or attack roll

Effect Kar cheers exuberantly, showering the triggering ally with fawning praise. The triggering ally gains 1d6 temporary Hit Points that last 1 minute.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.GGxPoUu7tcASRY0G]{Effect: Biggest Fan!}

My Turn!.name: My Turn!
My Turn!.description:
Requirements Kar used Biggest Fan within the last minute

Effect Eager to show she's just as strong as her ally, Kar gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks until the beginning of her next turn, but only if she performs some activity similar to the action that triggered her Biggest Fan! ability.

Flurry of Blows.name: Flurry of Blows
Flurry of Blows.description: Nakasha makes two unarmed Strikes. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

Flurry of Maneuvers.name: Flurry of Maneuvers
Flurry of Maneuvers.description: Nakasha can replace one or both of her attacks during a Flurry of Blows with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Grapples}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Shoves}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.ge56Lu1xXVFYUnLP]{Trips}.

Ironblood Stance.name: Ironblood Stance
Ironblood Stance.description: Nakasha enters a stance of impenetrable iron. As long as she's in this stance, Nakasha can make iron sweep Strikes with her fists and gains resistance 2 to all damage.

Powerful Fist.name: Powerful Fist
Powerful Fist.description: Nakasha's fist Strikes don't take penalties when making lethal attacks.

Stunning Fist.name: Stunning Fist
Stunning Fist.description: When Nakasha targets the same creature with two Strikes from her Flurry of Blows, she can try to stun the creature. If either Strike hits and deals damage, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1} (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 3} on a critical failure).

Stormblood Tiger.name: Stormblood Tiger
Stormblood Tiger.publicNotes:
Whereas most werecreatures follow the monthly rhythms of the full moon, the stormblood tiger's change is connected to the floods brought by annual rainfall. In parts of the world where flooding or heavy rains are both regular and predictable, such as during the Deluge in Belkzen, these lycanthropes stalk the land hunting for prey. Every night after the floods begin, a stormblood tiger is forced into its hybrid form until sun rises or the waters recede, usually 2–3 months later. The remainder of the year, stormblood tigers walk undetected among the population, often mistaken for hunters or scouts.

Sought Out

Because of the stormblood tiger's fearsome reputation and powerful abilities, some foolhardy orc warriors view becoming a stormblood tiger as a path to greatness—a blessing instead of a curse. In their minds, it's more important to have this power at hand during times of conflict than worry about disrupting peace during the Flood Truce. Wiser voices argue that too many stormblood tigers roaming about during the Flood Truce risks ending the accord altogether.

Tiger Empathy.name: Tiger Empathy
Tiger Empathy.description: The stormblood tiger can communicate with felines.

Change Shape.name: Change Shape
Change Shape.description:
Orc with fist +16 for 1d4+12 bludgeoning, or tiger with Speed 30 feet and Wrestle.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

Curse of the Stormblood Tiger.name: Curse of the Stormblood Tiger
Curse of the Stormblood Tiger.description:
This curse only affects humanoids.

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22]. Each night of flood season, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or transform into a stormblood tiger until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} until dawn.

Flood Frenzy.name: Flood Frenzy
Flood Frenzy.description: As the night falls in flood season, the stormblood tiger must enter hybrid form, can't Change Shape thereafter, becomes one size larger, increases its reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of its jaws Strike by 2. When the sun rises or the lands dry again, the stormblood tiger returns to humanoid form and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} for 24 hours.

Sudden Pounce.name: Sudden Pounce
Sudden Pounce.description: Imbued with an insatiable predatory instinct, stormblood tiger Leaps up to 15 feet and makes a claw Strike at the end of that movement. If the attack hits, the stormblood tiger can immediately Rend.

Wrestle.name: Wrestle
Wrestle.description: The tiger makes a claw Strike against a creature it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbing}. If the attack hits, that creature is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Trighoul.name: Trighoul
Trighoul.publicNotes:
Despite the name given by orcs of the Dirt Sea, the trighoul is not a ghoul at all, but a mutagenic abomination. Its three tentacles bristle with hundreds of neural spines that let it "glide" across the sands at high speed and manipulate the nervous system of a corpse through the spinal column. Trighouls were originally created over a thousand years ago by fleshwarpers aligned with the Whispering Tyrant, giving them to raiders as weapons. While the use of trighouls is now regarded with shame and disgust, some still take advantage of the creatures to lure enemies to their deaths using the corpses of their slain comrades.

Puppetmaster Serum

Orc alchemists familiar with fleshwarping techniques have synthesized an elixir called puppetmaster extract. The elixir transforms the drinker's limb into a tentacle that can temporarily animate the dead. One must take great care when crafting the serum, as incorrectly mixed batches have been known to dissolve organs from within, cause permanent paralysis, and grow tentacles in unwanted places.

Pain Sensitivity.name: Pain Sensitivity
Pain Sensitivity.description: When a trighoul's tentacle is severed, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. Its Speed is reduced by 15 feet until the tentacle is regrown.

Tentacle Regrowth.name: Tentacle Regrowth
Tentacle Regrowth.description: A trighoul ordinarily has three tentacles. A creature can attempt to sever a tentacle by specifically targeting it and dealing damage equal to the tentacle's Hit Points. A tentacle that isn't severed completely returns to full Hit Points at the end of any creature's turn. A severed trighoul tentacles regrows, restored to full Hit Points, after [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

Puppet Show.name: Puppet Show
Puppet Show.description:
1 to 3

Requirements The trighoul is using Puppeteer Corpse

Effect The trighoul manipulates up to three corpses to silently Impersonate the living. A creature that fails to detect the deception is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the trighoul's corpse Strike. The deception ends immediately after the trighoul attacks.

Puppeteer Corpse.name: Puppeteer Corpse
Puppeteer Corpse.description:
Requirements The trighoul is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}, and there's at least one corpse within 10 feet

Effect The trighoul attaches a tentacle to the corpse of a Large to Small creature. That tentacle replaces its Strike with a melee corpse Strike that deals bludgeoning damage equal to the total damage dealt by its tentacle. A controlled corpse is destroyed when it takes 20 Hit Points of damage. The trighoul can detach from a corpse as a single action with the manipulate trait.

Deny Advantage.name: Deny Advantage
Deny Advantage.description: An orc raider isn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to flanking, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}, or undetected creatures of 8th level or lower, or creatures of 8th level or lower using surprise attack. However, they can still help their allies flank.

Elemental Rage.name: Elemental Rage
Elemental Rage.description: While raging, an orc raider is cloaked in a vortex of fire, granting their weapons the versatile fire trait and granting them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealment} from ranged attacks. They can't use this concealment to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.XMcnh4cSI32tljXa]{Hide} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{Sneak}.

Destructive Inferno.name: Destructive Inferno
Destructive Inferno.description: While raging, an orc raider can channel their destructive energy into an explosion of flame. Creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:10]{10-foot radius} around the raider take @Damage[9d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic|options:area-effect] save). The raider can't use Destructive Inferno again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Destructive Inferno]]{1d4 rounds}.

Rage.name: Rage
Rage.description:
Requirements You aren't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} or raging.

You tap into your inner fury and begin raging. You gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to your level plus your Constitution modifier. While you are raging:

  • You deal 2 additional damage on melee Strikes. This additional damage is halved if your weapon or unarmed attack is agile.

  • You can't use actions with the concentrate trait unless they also have the rage trait. You can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} while raging.

Rage lasts for 1 minute, until you fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}, or until the encounter ends, whichever comes first. You can't voluntarily stop raging. When you stop raging, you lose any remaining temporary Hit Points from Rage, and can't gain temporary Hit Points from using the Rage action again for 1 minute.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.MaepXeSHiMoWDm7u]{Effect: Rage Temporary Hit Points Immunity}

Wyvern Bond.name: Wyvern Bond
Wyvern Bond.description: A wyvern rider automatically succeeds at checks to Command an Animal for a wyvern they're mounted on. While mounted, the wyvern acts on their turn.

Terrifying Strike.name: Terrifying Strike
Terrifying Strike.description: The wyvern rider attempts a weapon Strike against a target, shouting threats and curses. If it hits, the target must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:28] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 4} on a critical failure).

Weapon Adept.name: Weapon Adept
Weapon Adept.description: The wyvern rider can Swap between their falchion and composite shortbow, then Strike with the weapon they switched to.

Wyvern's Swoop.name: Wyvern's Swoop
Wyvern's Swoop.description:
Requirements The wyvern rider is mounting their wyvern

Effect The wyvern rider Commands their mount to Stride twice. At any point during this movement, they can Strike one enemy within reach or within the first range increment of their ranged weapon, with a +1 circumstance bonus to their attack roll. If they begin this movement at a higher point than their target, they deal an additional die of damage with this Strike.

Tusk Mountains Avalanche.name: Tusk Mountains Avalanche
Tusk Mountains Avalanche.disable: Three @Check[crafting|dc:34] or @Check[engineering-lore|dc:34] (expert) to create cover for a 10-foot square area and shelter from the avalanche; a flying creature can attempt a @Check[acrobatics|dc:29] check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Qf1ylAbdVi1rkc8M]{Maneuver in Flight} to avoid the avalanche
Tusk Mountains Avalanche.hazarddescription: Snow and ice gathered at the peaks of the mountain come crashing down in an explosive wave.

Cascade.name: Cascade
Cascade.description:
Trigger The zombie mammoth horde moves beneath the peaks

Effect An avalanche of snow and ice cascades down the mountainside at astonishing speed. All creatures on the ground within a 200-foot radius must attempt @Check[reflex|dc:30] saves.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[4d6[cold]] damage.

Failure The creature is swept away, moving 200 feet down the mountainside, and takes @Damage[8d6[bludgeoning]] damage and @Damage[8d6[cold]] damage.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is also buried, taking an additional @Damage[2d6[bludgeoning]] damage and then @Damage[1d6[cold]] damage per minute of burial. Locating a buried creature requires a @Check[perception|dc:28] or @Check[survival|dc:28] check; once located, a buried creature can be extricated with a @Check[athletics|dc:28] check in one round or dug out with a @Check[athletics|dc:23] check with 4 minutes of work.

Counting Coup.name: Counting Coup
Counting Coup.description:
Trigger An enemy moves within Otehika's reach

Effect Otehika swings their weapon as close to their enemy as they can without touching them, immediately attempting a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2u915NdUyQan6uKF]{Demoralize} check. On a success, the enemy is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the beginning of their next turn.

Twin Takedown.name: Twin Takedown
Twin Takedown.description:
Frequency once per round

Requirements Otehika wields two melee weapons, each in a different hand.

Effect Otehika makes two Strikes against their target, one with each of the required weapons. If both hit the same target, combine the damage from the weapons for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. They apply their multiple attack penalty to each Strike normally.

Army of Mist.name: Army of Mist
Army of Mist.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:28] (expert) to funnel the magic powering the mist back into itself, or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to exorcise some of the trapped souls and weaken the storm; three total successes in any combination are required to disable the haunt
Army of Mist.hazarddescription: The wind intensifies and the seeping fog rushes forward in a stampede of ghostly orcs screaming wordless battle cries, weapons raised to slaughter their foes.
Army of Mist.routine: (3 actions) The army of mist uses 3 actions to make frozen fists Strikes against three random creatures, favoring non-orcs or anyone who attempted to Disable it within the past round.
Army of Mist.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the area of Fort Whiteskull but resets immediately. The army of mist resets 1 day after being disabled if the ancestor storm isn't put to rest.

Battle Roar.name: Battle Roar
Battle Roar.description:
Trigger A creature enters the haunt's area

Effect The orc warriors roar in fury, dealing @Damage[(2d10+13)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save) to all living creatures within the haunt, then rolls initiative. Creatures that critically fail their saves are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} until Umbra's Last Stand is quelled.

Fattened War Pig.name: Fattened War Pig
Fattened War Pig.publicNotes:
While not native to Belkzen, these swine instead descended from livestock raided and re-homed millennia ago during orc raids. Generations of selective breeding favored larger animals suited to Belkzen's harsh environment and fearsome predators, resulting in the modern war pig. During the flood season, these pigs gorge and grow fat, gradually consuming their reserves to become leaner and meaner during the rest of the year.

Arguably domesticated, war pigs rarely remain in captivity for more than a few generations. Most orcs loathe the notion of their swine becoming too tame, instead releasing part of their herd every year to grow fierce before recapturing them seasons later. The downside is that these feral swine associate humanoids with food. Rogue war pigs sometimes form roving gangs that uproot fields, run amok, and even knock over trade caravans to eat rations (and the occasional merchant).

The "war" in war pigs comes from their use as mounts. With the right motivation, a war pig is a porcine juggernaut able to carry a fully armored orc into combat. Without discipline, care, and treats, that same pig can become 600 pounds of willful chaos that's as likely to trample foes as it is to flee combat, find a mud puddle, and crush its rider after flopping down to vigorously wallow.

Squealed Outrage.name: Squealed Outrage
Squealed Outrage.description:
Trigger The war pig is damaged by an adjacent creature

Effect The war pig makes a hoof Strike at the triggering creature.

Bulldoze.name: Bulldoze
Bulldoze.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The war pig Shoves an adjacent creature, after which it can either Stride or Step.

Fattened War Pig.name: Fattened War Pig
Fattened War Pig.description: The war pig gains a +2 status bonus to Athletics checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Shove} creatures.

Wallow.name: Wallow
Wallow.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The war pig rapidly rolls over. If it has a rider, the war pig @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.nZMQh4AaBr291TUf]{Bucks} as a free action. The war pig gains 1d8 temporary Hit Points for 1 minute and can then attempt an immediate flat check to recover from a single source of persistent acid, fire, or mental damage. If the war pig Wallows in an area with ample mud, water, loose soil, or similar material, the war pig instead gains 2d8 temporary Hit Points, for 1 minute and reduces the flat check DC to recover from persistent damage by 1.

The Carcass Man.name: The Carcass Man
The Carcass Man.allSaves: +2 status to all saves vs. fear and magic

+2 Status to All Saves vs. Fear and Magi.name: +2 Status to All Saves vs. Fear and Magic

Annihilation Aura.name: Annihilation Aura
Annihilation Aura.description:
20 feet. The Carcass Man's form exudes the impulse to destroy. Upon entering the aura, creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:31] save. Regardless of the results, creatures are temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success Urges to attack cloud the creature's mind and if they do not deal damage to another creature on their turn, they become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} until the end of their next turn.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} until the end of their next turn.

Critical Failure The creature is confused for 1 minute.

Neck Snap.name: Neck Snap
Neck Snap.description:
Requirements The Carcass Man has a creature Grabbed

Effect The creature takes @Damage[6d6[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:31|basic] save); a creature that critically fails their save is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} until they heal at least 30 HP. The Carcass Man then releases the creature.

Revel in Destruction.name: Revel in Destruction
Revel in Destruction.description:
Requirements The Carcass Man's last action reduced a creature to 0 HP

Effect The Carcass Man exults in his victory, painting his face in the creature's blood. He is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.nlCjDvLMf2EkV2dl]{Quickened} until the end of his next turn but can use this extra action only to Strike. Non-worshippers of Zagresh who witness the display must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:31] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

Three-Arm Devastation.name: Three-Arm Devastation
Three-Arm Devastation.description: The Carcass Man attempts a melee Strike against a creature within his reach. If the Strike hits and deals damage, he can attempt two additional melee Strikes with his other arms against two other creatures within his reach; each of the three Strikes must target a different creature. These attacks increase his multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until all three attacks have been made.

Shield Warden.name: Shield Warden
Shield Warden.description: When the shield bearer has their shield raised, they can Shield Block when an attack is made against an adjacent ally. If they do, the shield prevents that ally from taking damage instead of the shield bearer.

Offensive Block.name: Offensive Block
Offensive Block.description: The shield bearer can Step, make a shield spikes Strike, and Raise their Shield in any order.

Retributive Bash.name: Retributive Bash
Retributive Bash.description: The porphyry shield bearer deals an additional 1d8 damage with weapon Strikes against any creature that has damaged the shield bearer or an adjacent ally since the beginning of the shield bearer's last turn.

Tenacious Shield.name: Tenacious Shield
Tenacious Shield.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger The shield bearer's shield would be destroyed

Effect The shield is not destroyed and its Hit Points are instead reduced to 5.

Dance Between the Blades.name: Dance Between the Blades
Dance Between the Blades.description:
Trigger A foe within Paskis's reach critically fails a Strike against her

Effect Paskis can Step; she must end her movement within reach of the triggering foe. Paskis then makes a melee Strike against the triggering creature, treating them as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for the attack. This Strike uses the triggering Strike's damage rather than that of Paskis's weapon. If the weapon has the agile or finesse train, Paskis also applies her Sneak Attack to this damage.

Deny Advantage.name: Deny Advantage
Deny Advantage.description: Paskis isn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to creatures of 11th level or lower that are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}, undetected, flanking, or using surprise attack. However, she can help allies flank.

Enfeebling Strike.name: Enfeebling Strike
Enfeebling Strike.description:
Trigger Paskis's Strike hits an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creature and deals damage

Effect The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} until the end of Paskis's next turn.

Knife Training.name: Knife Training
Knife Training.description: Paskis treats all weapons in the knife group as finesse weapons.

Lightning Blades.name: Lightning Blades
Lightning Blades.description:
Requirements Paskis is wielding two knives and targets an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creature

Effect Paskis attempts two knife Strikes against the creature; these attacks increase her multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks. This counts as a single melee attack for the purposes of Feinting.

Mark for Death.name: Mark for Death
Mark for Death.description:
Requirements Paskis can observe the creature she intends to mark

Effect Paskis designates a single creature as her mark. This lasts until the mark dies or she uses Mark for Death again. She gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} the mark and on Deception checks to [[/act feint]] against the mark. In addition, her sneak attacks against her mark deal an additional 2 precision damage.

Poison Weapon.name: Poison Weapon
Poison Weapon.description: Paskis draws a poison and applies it to a weapon. If her next attack with that weapon before the end of her next turn hits and deals damage, it applies the effects of the poison.

Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
Sneak Attack.description: Paskis deals an extra 3d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

Surprise Attack.name: Surprise Attack
Surprise Attack.description: On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to Paskis.

Slow.name: Slow
Slow.description: A zombie brown bear is permanently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use reactions.

Ripping Maul.name: Ripping Maul
Ripping Maul.description:
Requirements The zombie brown bear has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}

Effect The zombie chews and rips at the target, dealing @Damage[(2d8+5)[piercing]] damage with a @Check[type:reflex|dc:17|basic] save.

Note: A DC was not provided for this ability by Paizo. The DC present here is a moderate DC for the creature level according to the GM Core creature building Tables.

Orc Vagabond.name: Orc Vagabond
Orc Vagabond.publicNotes:
Tough and no-nonsense, orc vagabonds are just as likely to serve their own interests as they are those of a warlord. Some live as bandit leaders, assembling their own group of veterans and outcasts. Many others work as the elite guards of their hold, keeping a watchful eye out for trouble.

The orcs of Belkzen are survivors to the core. Shaped by the unforgiving landscape, they're experts at managing resources and making do with scarcity. Aside from the relative peace from late spring through early summer during the Flood Truce, Belkzen's orc holds are nearly always at odds with each other, though these conflicts rarely rise to the level of war. Instead, there are raids for supplies, skirmishes to establish borders or retake lost territory, and feuds over personal slights. Outsiders who travel in Belkzen often become embroiled in these conflicts; sometimes they even trigger the fights themselves.

The members of a given hold are often quite spread out, rather than centralized, so communication and coordination is sporadic. Even the Empty Hand orcs of Urgir rarely stay in the city all year long. At the very least, they travel a few miles to the Flood Road when the aurochs arrive, and they may wander even further afield when the mood strikes.

Orc Faith

With so many sudden disruptions to the orc pantheon, the faithful find themselves in a confusing time of change. Fervent worshippers of Lanishra, Sezelrian, and Zagresh don't particularly like the new deities who have usurped their old gods. Some of these zealots desperately cling to their old faiths, and many still hold a sliver of their fallen deity's power, further adding to the confusion.

Salvage

Orcs are experts at repurposing materials for their own use. Belkzen doesn't have rich soil or abundant deposits of valuable metals and minerals. Instead, orc crafters rely on salvage from battles or trade with outsiders for their raw materials. It's common for a basic tool, such as a knife or cooking pot, to be very old yet kept in perfect working condition. Even when a tool breaks, orc crafters find ways to reuse as much of it as they can manage.

Surprise Shooter.name: Surprise Shooter
Surprise Shooter.description: The orc vagabond's ranged attacks deal an extra 1d6 precision damage to targets adjacent to at least one of the vagabond's allies.

Stonegutter Mold.name: Stonegutter Mold
Stonegutter Mold.disable: @Check[survival|dc:26] (trained) to scrape away the mold without touching it
Stonegutter Mold.hazarddescription: A hardy, innocuous-looking green mold grows over surfaces, slowly absorbing minerals.
Stonegutter Mold.reset: 1 hour, as the mold settles back across the surface.

Calcium Consumption.name: Calcium Consumption
Calcium Consumption.description:
Trigger A creature with bones touches the mold or steps into an area covered with it

Effect The mold shudders and slithers onto the triggering creature as it attempts to absorb calcium through their skin. The creature attempts a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:25] save.

Critical Success The target shakes the mold off and is unaffected.

Success A small patch of mold clings to the target and deals @Damage[(2d10+9)[piercing]] damage.

Failure The mold clings to the target. The target takes @Damage[(4d10+18)[piercing]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

Critical Failure The mold envelops the target. As failure, except the target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} until it's healed to its Hit Point maximum.

Note: A DC was not provided for this ability by Paizo. The DC present here is a hard DC for the hazard level according to the GM Core hazard building Tables.

Nighttime Conflagration.name: Nighttime Conflagration
Nighttime Conflagration.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:27] to beat out the flames, @Check[crafting|dc:27] to rig up a firefighting device, or @Check[nature|dc:27] to cut off avenues of the fire's spread. On a success, one square of the fire is extinguished (two squares on a critical success). Dousing a square in water, such as with create water, automatically extinguishes that square. When all squares are extinguished, the hazard ends.
Nighttime Conflagration.routine: (1+ actions) Each square of flames spreads to an adjacent square, taking one action per square. Creatures adjacent to the fire take @Damage[3d6[fire]] damage each round from the heat (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save); a creature who fails their save is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} by the smoke (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). If there are more than 10 active squares of flames, creatures adjacent to the fire instead take @Damage[6d6[fire]] damage each round (@Check[fortitude|dc:30|basic] save), and a creature who fails their save is sickened 2 (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 3} on a critical failure). A creature standing in the flames takes @Damage[12d6[fire]] damage each round (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic] save); a creature who fails their save also takes @Damage[4d6[persistent,fire]] damage.
Nighttime Conflagration.stealthdetails: (trained) (decreases by 2 each round the hazard is active) to smell the smoke and hear the crackling flames

Growing Blaze.name: Growing Blaze
Growing Blaze.description:
Trigger The arsonists set the fire

Effect The hay around the initial flames catches, setting two 5-foot squares in the affected room on fire. The hazard rolls initiative.

Opportunistic Strike.name: Opportunistic Strike
Opportunistic Strike.description:
Trigger A creature within the hold sergeant's melee reach is hit by a melee attack by one of the hold sergeant's allies

Effect The hold sergeant makes a Strike against the triggering creature.

Bark Orders.name: Bark Orders
Bark Orders.description:
Bellowing mightily, the hold sergeant gives themself and all orc allies within 60 feet a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls until the start of the hold sergeant's next turn.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.PvOaRjOnUrPkfbxe]{Effect: Bark Orders (Hold Sergeant)}

Intimidating Strike.name: Intimidating Strike
Intimidating Strike.description: Murshek makes a melee Strike. If she hits and deals damage, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical hit.

Toxic Teamwork.name: Toxic Teamwork
Toxic Teamwork.description: Murshek's attacks exacerbate any venom in her target's system. Against a creature affected by wyvern venom, her Strikes deal additional poison damage equal to twice the current stage of the wyvern venom affecting the target.

Wingrider Trust.name: Wingrider Trust
Wingrider Trust.description: Murshek and Xithnik operate as a team and verbally coordinate their actions. Xithnik has 3 actions, and Murshek does not need to spend any actions to command him.

Orc Spinecracker.name: Orc Spinecracker
Orc Spinecracker.publicNotes:
Orc spinecrackers are skilled in the art of hand-to-hand combat, forgoing weapons in favor of gauntlets or bare knuckles.

While some orc warriors specialize in a technique of their hold, others synthesize styles from all over into a unified yet versatile technique.

Orc Duels

Dueling is a frequent practice among many cultures of Golarion, and orcs are no exception. The lethality of these duels varies based on the duel's impetus—a friendly bout goes to first blood or until a combatant is knocked out, while a fight in response to a vendetta or insult is more likely to the death. Unlike in many dueling traditions, spectators are encouraged to verbally aid combatants, shouting out encouragement to one's preferred fighter and invectives to their opponent.

Brawler Critical Specialization.name: Brawler Critical Specialization
Brawler Critical Specialization.description: When the orc spinecracker gets a critical hit with a brawling weapon, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} until the end of the spinecracker's next turn.

Combat Grab.name: Combat Grab
Combat Grab.description:
Requirements The spinecracker has one hand free, and their target is within reach of that hand

Effect Using their prior attack to shift their opponent's guard, the spinecracker makes a melee Strike with one hand. If it hits, they grab the target using their other hand. The creature remains @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} until the end of the spinecracker's next turn or until it Escapes, whichever comes first.

Crushing Pin.name: Crushing Pin
Crushing Pin.description:
Requirements The spinecracker has a creature Grappled

Effect The spinecracker crushes the creature in their grip, dealing @Damage[(2d6+6)[bludgeoning]] damage and giving the target the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} condition until the end of the spinecracker's next turn.

Bristle Boar.name: Bristle Boar
Bristle Boar.publicNotes:
The wild animals that have adapted to the harsh environs of Belkzen are either fierce enough to scare off predators, tough enough to survive attacks, or smart enough to avoid trouble. Bristle boars are all three. Many orcs admire the bristle boar, viewing them as living symbols of the toughness Belkzen demands from its inhabitants. With their spiny manes, sharp tusks, keen noses, and irascible nature, the creatures are both intimidating and dangerous.

Hunters throughout the Inner Sea region know that taking down a wild boar can prove deadly for the unprepared. Bristle boars—the name given to both male and female specimen—are even more challenging to kill. Further, their meat is sinewy, so few hunters even bother with the animals. For those who fell the beasts, the boars' spined hides often serve as trophies of the hunter's skill.

Bristle boars are not as reliant on the Deluge as other animals in Belkzen.
