Though they're certainly found in the green areas that spring up during the annual flooding, sounders of bristle boars can survive even in desolate areas like the Dirt Sea, grubbing for sustenance where few others can. The Cleft Head Hold maintains a small herd of tamed bristle boars.

The orcs have learned that, with the right trainer, the clever animals make excellent companions. Aside from their utility in battle, the boars are often used to sniff out the rare yellow truffles that grow throughout the hold.

A typical bristle boar stands 4 feet tall and weighs roughly 200 pounds.

Stuck Treasure

Although they don't seek out shiny objects like some animals, bristle boars who evade hunters often escape with weapons embedded in their thick hides. The Empty Hand orcs tell of a famous bristle boar dubbed "Silverspear" after the silvery spear stuck in its hide. Stories claim the boar's spear is imbued with powerful magic, and while many warriors have hunted Silverspear to claim its weapon, so far none have succeeded.

Bristle Spines.name: Bristle Spines
Bristle Spines.description: When an adjacent creature hits the bristle boar with a melee attack, that creature takes @Damage[1d6[piercing]] damage unless it succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:17] save.

Boar Charge.name: Boar Charge
Boar Charge.description: The bristle boar Strides twice and then makes a tusk Strike. As long as it moved at least 20 feet, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to its attack roll.

Dustup.name: Dustup
Dustup.description:
Requirements The bristle boar is standing in an area with soil, dust, sand, or similar debris

Frequency once per round

Effect The bristle boar spins and kicks up dirt, knocking it into foes' faces. Each adjacent creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save.

Success The creature is unaffected and immune to the bristle boar's Dustup for 24 hours.

Success The creature unaffected.

Failure The creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} and dazzled for 1 round.

Flood Ghoul.name: Flood Ghoul
Flood Ghoul.publicNotes:
Ghouls stalkers are ravenous undead who haunt graveyards and eat corpses.

Few creatures are more ubiquitous to sinister locations such as lonely graveyards and ruined crypts than the flesh-eating undead known as ghouls.

Consume Flesh.name: Consume Flesh
Consume Flesh.description:
Requirements The flood ghoul is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour

Effect The flood ghoul devours a chunk of the corpse and regains @Damage[2d6[healing]] Hit Points. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

Death Echo.name: Death Echo
Death Echo.description:
Requirements The flood ghoul has damaged a creature with a melee attack

Effect The flood ghoul unleashes a wave of memories of its death by drowning. The targeted creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:15] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed} for 1 round as they relive the flood ghoul's dying moments. A creature who successfully saves becomes temporarily immune for 1 hour.

Forbidden Cravings.name: Forbidden Cravings
Forbidden Cravings.description:
A creature can still eat and drink while sickened by this curse

Saving Throw @Check[will|dc:18]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effects (1 day)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d6[void]] damage and the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} until it consumes raw meat (1 day)

Stage 3 as stage 2

Stage 4 as stage 2 unless the target has consumed raw meat in the past 24 hours; then it takes @Damage[4d6[void]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} until it consumes raw meat

Stage 5 If the creature has eaten raw meat in the past 24 hours, it dies and rises as a ghoul; if not, it returns to stage 4 .

Ghoul Whispers.name: Ghoul Whispers
Ghoul Whispers.description:
Requirements A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} creature is within the ghoul's reach

Effect The ghoul whispers dark thoughts and vile cravings into the creature's ears. The creature must attempt a save against the forbidden cravings curse.

Swift Leap.name: Swift Leap
Swift Leap.description: The ghoul jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

Shadefield Spirit.name: Shadefield Spirit
Shadefield Spirit.publicNotes:
The ghost commoner is an ordinary person who believes they died unjustly.

When some mortals die through tragic circumstances or without closure, their souls can linger and haunt a locale significant to them in life.

Site Bound.name: Site Bound
Site Bound.description:
The Shadefield spirit is bound to the Shadefields.

A typical ghost can stray only a short distance from where it was killed or the place it haunts. A typical limit is 120 feet. Some ghosts are instead bound to a room, building, item, or creature that was special to it rather than a location.

Rejuvenation.name: Rejuvenation
Rejuvenation.description:
Putting Diskah Night Watcher's spirit to rest allows the Shadefield spirit to return to its original role as a guardian haunt.

When a ghost is destroyed, it re-forms after [[/br 2d4 #days]]{2d4 days} within the location it's bound to, fully healed. A ghost can be permanently destroyed only if someone determines the reason for its existence and sets right whatever prevents the spirit from resting.

Cindery Assault.name: Cindery Assault
Cindery Assault.description: The Shadefield spirit cries out in pain and anguish as flaming objects and debris fly about in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30]. Creatures in this area take @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning],1d6[persistent,fire]|options:area-damage]{3d6 bludgeoning damage plus 1d6 persistent fire damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|options:area-effect] save).

Corrupting Gaze.name: Corrupting Gaze
Corrupting Gaze.description: The Shadefield spirit stares at a creature it can see within 30 feet. The target takes @Damage[3d6[void]] damage (@Check[will|dc:20|basic] save). A creature that fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute.

Sworn to the Storm.name: Sworn to the Storm
Sworn to the Storm.description: The Shadefield spirit gains a +1 circumstance bonus to attacks against creatures that have attempted to Disable the Shoanti ancestor storm.

Half-Ear's Luck.name: Half-Ear's Luck
Half-Ear's Luck.description:
Frequency once per day

Trigger Zdagren takes a critical hit or critically fails a saving throw

Effect The critical hit becomes a normal hit, or the critical failure becomes a normal failure.

Double Slice.name: Double Slice
Double Slice.description: Zdagren makes two Strikes against the same target, one with each of his knuckle daggers. Zdagren combines the damage of any attacks that hit and applies precision damage, resistances, and weaknesses only once. Both attacks count toward his multiple attack penalty, but the penalty increases only after both attacks.

Formation Command.name: Formation Command
Formation Command.description: Each ally within 30 feet of Zdagren can Step, or Stride up to half their Speed. Each ally can benefit from Formation Command only once per round.

Aurochs Herd.name: Aurochs Herd
Aurochs Herd.publicNotes:
Massive aurochs herds roam the badlands of Belkzen, grazing upon the limited grass and plant life dispersed throughout the wasteland's valleys. Having adapted to the arid mountain terrain, aurochs herds eat a much more diverse diet of scrub and brush than their domesticated counterparts as they steadily migrate down the Flood Road. The herds show little patience for predators and demonstrate a preference for charging threats with their razor-sharp horns. Bull aurochs display natural instincts to strategically protect other, weaker members of the herd, often surprising intruders with their coordinated tactics and defensive maneuvers.

Unlikely War Beasts

Followers of Zagresh have a history of capturing wild aurochs for use in magical experiments, taking the strongest of a herd to make the beasts even larger and more aggressive. Once the aurochs have been transformed into monstrous goliaths, their captors bard them with armored plates and magical weapons that complement their horns. In battle, these aurochs are utilized to pull siege weapons, break through enemy defenses, and trample frontline forces.

Stampede.name: Stampede
Stampede.description:
Trigger The aurochs herd's Hit Points drops below a threshold

Effect The aurochs herd uses Trample but moves in a direction directly opposite of the threat; the DC increases to @Check[reflex|dc:27|basic] and the bludgeoning damage increases to @Damage[(4d6+6)[bludgeoning]] (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} creatures take @Damage[(5d6+6)[bludgeoning]] damage instead).

Circle of Horns.name: Circle of Horns
Circle of Horns.description: The largest males in the herd form a ring of protection around the more vulnerable members. The herd gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of its next turn.

Horn.name: Horn
Horn.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The aurochs herd makes a melee attack against each enemy in a @Template[emanation|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save). The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(2d8+8)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d8+10)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(2d10+9)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

Puncturing Charge.name: Puncturing Charge
Puncturing Charge.description:
Requirements The aurochs herd has formed a Circle of Horns

Effect The aurochs herd Strides to an enemy and makes a Horn Strike, dealing @Damage[(3d8+8)[piercing]] damage plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.LbnsT8yXchHMQT3t]{Improved Knockdown}.

Trample.name: Trample
Trample.description:
Huge or smaller, hoof (@Damage[(3d6+6)[bludgeoning]] damage; @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} creatures take @Damage[(4d6+6)[bludgeoning]] damage), @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save (any creature that fails its save is knocked prone).

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Trample]

Troop Movement.name: Troop Movement
Troop Movement.description: Whenever the aurochs herd Strides, it first Forms Up as a free action to condense into a 20-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares,) and then moves up to its Speed. This works just like a Gargantuan creature moving; for instance, if any of the herd's squares enter difficult terrain, the extra movement cost applies to the whole troop.

Moonstalker.name: Moonstalker
Moonstalker.publicNotes:
The most tenacious hunters of the Lyrune-Quah track and kill nocturnal beasts under the light of the moon. When such a warrior perishes during the hunt, sometimes their spirit lingers and transforms into a specter of the night, refusing to move on until their prey is slain.

Loyal in Death

Though moonstalkers retain few memories of life, their connections to their quah resonate with them even after their demise. Moonstalkers have been seen protecting living Shoanti warriors who make camp within their hunting grounds, especially warbands that show reverence for the hunt. Outsiders are rarely, if ever, extended the same courtesy.

Site Bound.name: Site Bound
Site Bound.description: The moonstalker can stray no more than 120 feet from the hunting grounds where it was slain.

Cover of Night.name: Cover of Night
Cover of Night.description: A moonstalker in an area illuminated by moonlight gains a +2 status bonus to AC and Stealth checks. In the light of a full moon, they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}.

Lunar Shift.name: Lunar Shift
Lunar Shift.description:
Trigger The moonstalker is hit by a Strike

Requirements The moonstalker is in an area illuminated by moonlight

Effect The moonstalker teleports through a beam of moonlight to another moonlit space within 120 feet. This does not trigger reactions.

Rejuvenation.name: Rejuvenation
Rejuvenation.description:
Slaying the moonstalker's prey from their final hunt allows them to move on to the afterlife.

When a ghost is destroyed, it re-forms after [[/br 2d4 #days]]{2d4 days} within the location it's bound to, fully healed. A ghost can be permanently destroyed only if someone determines the reason for its existence and sets right whatever prevents the spirit from resting.

Corrupting Gaze.name: Corrupting Gaze
Corrupting Gaze.description: The moonstalker stares at a creature it can see within 30 feet. The target takes @Damage[4d6[void]] damage (@Check[will|dc:25|basic] save). A creature that fails its save is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute.

Hunt Prey.name: Hunt Prey
Hunt Prey.description: The moonstalker designates a single creature they can see and hear, or one they're Tracking, as their prey. The moonstalker gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} the prey and to Survival checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.EA5vuSgJfiHH7plD]{Track} the prey. The first time the moonstalker hits the designated prey in a round, they deal an additional 1d8 precision damage. The moonstalker also ignores the penalty for making ranged attacks within their second range increment. These effects last until the moonstalker uses Hunt Prey again.

Soul Pierce.name: Soul Pierce
Soul Pierce.description: The moonstalker infuses soul-rending energy into a spectral arrow, making a ghostly composite longbow Strike with a –2 circumstance penalty against each creature in a @Template[type:line|distance:100].

Locus of Death.name: Locus of Death
Locus of Death.disable: @Check[nature|dc:27] (master) to infuse the locus with life force, @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to counteract the locus's energies, @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to extricate the orb from the altar
Locus of Death.hazarddescription: A magical orb inflicts terrible visions of gruesome ends on anyone who approaches.
Locus of Death.routine: (1 action; death, emotion, fear, mental) One living creature in the haunt's location (determined randomly) is targeted by a 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Jmxru8zMdYMRuO5n]{Vision of Death} (@Check[will|dc:32|traits:death,emotion,fear,mental]). A creature that critically fails their save doesn't physically flee but instead can't attempt to disable the hazard until they're no longer frightened. Once all creatures have been affected, the haunt deactivates.
Locus of Death.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates.

Death Grip.name: Death Grip
Death Grip.description:
Trigger A creature touches the orb or attempts to disable the haunt

Effect Necromantic energy lashes out from the orb and the triggering creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:32] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

Death Manifest.name: Death Manifest
Death Manifest.description: Casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Hnc7eGi7vyZenAIm]{Breath of Life}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.YvXKGlHOt7mdW2jZ]{Death Ward}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.IkGYwHRLhkuoGReG]{Raise Dead} in the orb's presence automatically destroys the locus; other spells or abilities that restore the dead to life might also do so, at GM discretion.

Unholy Repair.name: Unholy Repair
Unholy Repair.description: The locus is sustained by the power of Tar-Baphon. The orb regains 10 Hit Points at the beginning of every round and is repaired by void damage.

Blessing of the Land.name: Blessing of the Land
Blessing of the Land.description: As long as she's within the boundaries of the Shadefields, Diskah treats all terrain as one difficulty less, for example difficult terrain has no effect, and she treats greater difficult terrain as difficult terrain.

Ancestral Fury.name: Ancestral Fury
Ancestral Fury.description: Diskah cries out in grief and rage. All creatures within a 30-foot radius must attempt a @Check[will|dc:28] save or have the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition for 1 round. Diskah can't use Ancestral Fury again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Ancestral Fury]]{1d4 rounds}.

Lunar Blaze.name: Lunar Blaze
Lunar Blaze.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Diskah calls out to the moon and is wreathed in fiery white moonlight for 1 minute. While this ability is active, Diskah is immune to fire damage and her melee Strikes deal an additional 1d6 persistent fire damage. Any creature that hits her with a melee attack or otherwise touches her takes @Damage[1d8[fire]] damage.

Spirit's Blessing.name: Spirit's Blessing
Spirit's Blessing.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Diskah loosens one of the plaits in her braid and regains one 4th-rank spell slot she can use to cast any of her prepared 4th-rank spells.

Snow Vision.name: Snow Vision
Snow Vision.description: Bugark ignores the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} condition from falling snow.

Reactive Strike.name: Reactive Strike
Reactive Strike.description:
Bugark can use Reactive Strike when a creature within his reach uses a concentrate action, in addition to its normal trigger. He can disrupt triggering concentrate actions, and it disrupts actions on any hit, not just a critical hit.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ReactiveStrike]

Frozen Breath.name: Frozen Breath
Frozen Breath.description: Bugark exhales a @Template[type:cone|distance:60] of razor-sharp ice that deals @Damage[6d6[cold],5d6[slashing]|options:area-damage]{6d6 cold damage and 5d6 slashing} damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save). Bugark can't use Frozen Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Frozen Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

Intimidating Strike.name: Intimidating Strike
Intimidating Strike.description: Bugark makes a melee Strike. If he hits and deals damage, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical hit.

Smite.name: Smite
Smite.description: Bugark chooses one enemy he can see. Until the start of his next turn, his Strikes against that foe using his maul deal an extra 6 spirit damage with the unholy trait. If the chosen enemy attacks him before the start of his next turn, the duration extends to the end of that enemy's next turn. If the enemy continues to attack him, the duration continues to extend.

Rider's Bond.name: Rider's Bond
Rider's Bond.description: Bloodwing acts on Molog's initiative while Molog is mounted. Molog automatically succeeds at attempts to Command her and doesn't need to spend actions to do so, but Bloodwing only has two actions per turn as long as Molog is mounted.

Savage.name: Savage
Savage.description:
Trigger A creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by Bloodwing critically fails a skill check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape}

Effect Bloodwing makes a stinger Strike against the triggering creature.

Powerful Dive.name: Powerful Dive
Powerful Dive.description: Bloodwing Flies up to her fly Speed and must both move forward at least 20 feet and descend at least 10 feet. If she ends the movement within melee reach of at least one enemy her size or smaller, she can make a claw Strike against that enemy. If the claw hits, as a free action Bloodwing can either automatically Grab the target or knock it @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Punishing Momentum.name: Punishing Momentum
Punishing Momentum.description:
Requirements Bloodwing @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} a creature this turn using Powerful Dive

Effect Bloodwing can Fly at half Speed while holding the creature in her claws, carrying that creature along with her and dropping it at the end of her movement. Alternatively, Bloodwing can Strike the creature with her stinger with a +2 circumstance bonus.

Wyvern Poison.name: Wyvern Poison
Wyvern Poison.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:25]

Maximum Duration 6 rounds

Stage 1 @Damage[3d6[poison]] damage plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @Damage[3d8[poison]] damage plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} (1 round)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d10[poison]] damage plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 3} (1 round)

Umbra's Rage.name: Umbra's Rage
Umbra's Rage.disable: @Check[religion|dc:26] (expert) to exorcise the closest spirits or @Check[diplomacy|dc:30] (expert) to calm them down
Umbra's Rage.hazarddescription: The anger of the fallen Umbra Legion seeps into any creature entering combat.

Curse of Eternal Battle.name: Curse of Eternal Battle
Curse of Eternal Battle.description:
Trigger A creature enters combat while in Umbra's Last Stand

Effect The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save as the rage of the surrounding orcs threatens to overwhelm them.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is confused for 1 minute.

Slamming Gate.name: Slamming Gate
Slamming Gate.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:26] (trained) to jam either the floor panel or the associated winch
Slamming Gate.hazarddescription: A pressure-sensitive floor panel causes a spiked door to slam down into place from the ceiling, skewering anyone caught underneath.

Slam Closed.name: Slam Closed
Slam Closed.description:
Trigger Pressure is placed on the associated floor tile

Effect The door falls, closing off the hallway. The door deals @Damage[(3d8+3)[bludgeoning],(3d8+3)[piercing]]{3d8+3 bludgeoning damage and 3d8+3 piercing} damage to anyone beneath or adjacent to the door when it falls, and it pushes them out of its space (away from the door, or in a random direction if the creature is directly beneath the door) as it falls. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save takes no damage and moves out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, they choose the direction.

Lifting the closed door requires a successful @Check[athletics|dc:25] check. Hitting the floor panels triggers the trap. The slab uses the same AC and saves as the trap, but it has Hardness 8 and 60 HP (BT 30).

Ferocity.name: Ferocity
Ferocity.description:
Trigger The hold warrior is reduced to 0 HP

Effect The hold warrior avoids being knocked out and remains at 1 HP, but their @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded} value increases by 1. When they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded 3}, they can no longer use this ability.

Collapsing Cliff Stairs.name: Collapsing Cliff Stairs
Collapsing Cliff Stairs.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:25] or Engineering Lore to shore up the supports, @Check[perception|dc:27] to notice the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} bypass lever in the stair railing, or @Check[thievery|dc:29] to disrupt the connecting mechanism
Collapsing Cliff Stairs.hazarddescription: The stairs' supports are rigged to collapse if the trip wires are triggered, sending a 10-foot section of stairs tumbling down the cliff.

Structural Collapse.name: Structural Collapse
Structural Collapse.description:
Trigger The trip wires are pulled or severed

Effect The wires pull away the mechanisms supporting the stairs, causing them to collapse. Each creature standing on the affected area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:29] save.

Critical Success The creature jumps safely to the cliff and is unaffected.

Success The creature jumps to the cliff, but takes @Damage[10[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage from the leap.

Failure The creature falls to the next section of stairs, 100 feet down the cliff, and takes @Damage[50[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage. They can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.3yoajuKjwHZ9ApUY]{Grab an Edge} to avoid falling.

Critical Failure As failure, but the fall distance is 200 feet, all the way down the cliff, dealing @Damage[100[bludgeoning]|options:fall-damage] damage.

Wraithwell.name: Wraithwell
Wraithwell.disable: @Check[religion|dc:27] to negate the forces of undeath (master), @Check[occultism|dc:30] (expert) to channel vital energy, @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to extricate the orb from the altar
Wraithwell.hazarddescription: An orb of undeath draws creatures closer to a cursed existence.
Wraithwell.routine: (1 action; void) The wraiths attack all living creatures in the area, dealing @Damage[(3d10+14)[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:32|basic] save). Creatures who fail their saves are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} on a critical failure) or increase their drained value by 1 (2 on a critical failure), up to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 4}.
Wraithwell.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates.

Unholy Repair.name: Unholy Repair
Unholy Repair.description: The locus is sustained by the power of Tar-Baphon. The orb regains 10 Hit Points at the beginning of every round and is repaired by void damage.

Wraith Storm.name: Wraith Storm
Wraith Storm.description:
Trigger A creature touches the orb or attempts to disable the haunt

Effect A cyclone of wraiths spirals from the orb, grasping at all living creatures in the area. Targeted creatures must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:32] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 2} on a critical failure).

Undeath Manifest.name: Undeath Manifest
Undeath Manifest.description: A creature who dies in the presence of the orb immediately becomes undead. Casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.GUeRTriJkMlMlVrk]{Bind Undead} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.xRgU9rrhmGAgG4Rc]{Peaceful Rest} in the presence of the orb automatically destroys the locus; other spells or abilities that combat undead might also do so, at GM discretion.

Locus of Destruction.name: Locus of Destruction
Locus of Destruction.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:27] (master) to reconfigure the locus, @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] (expert) to counteract the locus's energies, @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to extricate the orb from the altar
Locus of Destruction.hazarddescription: A magical orb drives intruders to commit acts of mindless destruction.
Locus of Destruction.routine:
(1 action; mental) The destructive impulses grow stronger. Affected creatures must attempt a @Check[will|dc:32|traits:mental] save; creatures who fail or critically fail can't attempt to disable the haunt that turn.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected this round.

Failure The creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round, targeting both creatures and objects.

Critical Failure As failure but the confusion lasts for 1 minute.
Locus of Destruction.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates.

Destruction's Fury.name: Destruction's Fury
Destruction's Fury.description:
Trigger A creature touches the orb or attempts to disable the haunt

Effect A destructive fury fills the minds of all living creatures in the area, who must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:32] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Destruction Manifest.name: Destruction Manifest
Destruction Manifest.description: Casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.TUj8eugNqAvB1vVR]{Creation} or a creation domain spell (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.xTpp8dHZsNMDm75B]{Creative Splash} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.eb4FXf62NYArTqek]{Artistic Flourish}) in the orb's presence automatically destroys the locus; other spells or abilities that create things might also do so at GM discretion.

Unholy Repair.name: Unholy Repair
Unholy Repair.description: The locus is sustained by the power of Tar-Baphon. The orb regains 10 Hit Points at the beginning of every round and is repaired by void damage.

Destructive Backlash.name: Destructive Backlash
Destructive Backlash.description:
Trigger The orb is damaged by an attack

Effect The locus makes a destructive lash Strike against the triggering creature.

Counting Coup.name: Counting Coup
Counting Coup.description:
Trigger An enemy moves within the Sklar-Quah warrior's reach

Effect The Sklar-Quah warrior swings their weapon as close to their enemy as they can without touching them, immediately attempting a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2u915NdUyQan6uKF]{Demoralize} check. On a success, the enemy is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the beginning of their next turn.

Guerrilla Tactics.name: Guerrilla Tactics
Guerrilla Tactics.description:
Requirements The Sklar-Quah warrior is holding their hatchet

Effect The Sklar-Quah warrior makes a ranged Strike with their hatchet and then draws their battle axe.

Floodslain Wolf.name: Floodslain Wolf
Floodslain Wolf.publicNotes:
The sudden, crashing waters of the Deluge kill many creatures in Belkzen each year. The surprise and terror these victims of the Deluge feel during their last moments is sometimes enough to raise them from the dead as floodslain. As the waters that killed them recede, the undead seek others to join them. Despite their broken, shattered bodies, floodslain are often recognizable to those who knew them in life.

Floodslain creatures are rare outside Belkzen, leading some scholars to speculate that the waters of the Deluge are somehow cursed. Others believe the proximity of Gallowspire could be the cause. If so, this means the Gravelands could soon see floodslain arise there too, as the Whispering Tyrant furthers his plots after escaping that prison.

Panicked Eyes

One distinctive feature all floodslain share are their wide, panic-stricken eyes. A floodslain's gaze is permanently frozen in the expression of terror the creature felt moments before its death. Some floodslain are rumored to have the ability to spread this fear to others with a mere glance.

In Search of Water

Floodslain tend to move toward lower ground, instinctively seeking water. Belkzen's flat floodplains, however, mean that the creatures wander in unpredictable ways. A common saying among old orc crafters is "build on high, keep dry," but many add "build low, the dead flow."

Drowning Touch.name: Drowning Touch
Drowning Touch.description:
The target's lungs begin to fill with water and they must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:17] save. A target affected by Drowning Touch can spend a single action coughing in an attempt to recover, which immediately lets them attempt a new Fortitude save against the effect. A success improves the previous result by 1 step. A critical success improves the previous result by 2, and a critical failure decreases it by 1.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is slowed 2 and drowning.

Floodslain Spawn.name: Floodslain Spawn
Floodslain Spawn.description: A living animal or humanoid killed by a floodslain creature will rise as a floodslain if their corpse is left in water for 24 hours. The new floodslain isn't under the control of the floodslain creature that killed it.

Pack Attack.name: Pack Attack
Pack Attack.description: The floodslain wolf's Strikes deal 1d4 extra damage to creatures within reach of at least two of the floodslain wolf's allies.

Sodden Ground.name: Sodden Ground
Sodden Ground.description: 20 feet. Water flows endlessly from a floodslain creature, making the area around it slippery. The ground in the aura is difficult terrain for all non-floodslain creatures.

Vomit Flotsam.name: Vomit Flotsam
Vomit Flotsam.description: The floodslain vomits flotsam in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15]. Any creature in the area takes @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic|options:area-effect] save).

Locus of Pain.name: Locus of Pain
Locus of Pain.disable: @Check[medicine|dc:27] (master) to soothe the locus's pain, @Check[occultism|dc:30] or @Check[religion|dc:30] to counteract the locus's energies, @Check[thievery|dc:35] (master) to extricate the orb from the altar
Locus of Pain.hazarddescription: An arcane orb sits upon a sinister altar, filling creatures in the area with unimaginable pain.
Locus of Pain.routine: (1 action; mental, nonlethal) Creatures affected by the haunt's agony lash are wracked with pain, as 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.R8bqnYiThB6MYTxD]{Phantom Pain} (@Check[will|dc:32|traits:mental,nonlethal]). If a creature falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} due to the haunt, they don't continue taking damage.
Locus of Pain.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the area. After 1 hour, the haunt reactivates.

Agony Lash.name: Agony Lash
Agony Lash.description:
Trigger A creature touches the orb or attempts to disable the haunt

Effect All living creatures within 60 feet are overwhelmed by pain and must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:32] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The haunt then rolls initiative.

Pain Manifest.name: Pain Manifest
Pain Manifest.description: Casting @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.b515AZlB0sridKSq]{Calm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.szIyEsvihc5e1w8n]{Soothe} in the orb's presence automatically destroys the locus; other spells or abilities that ease pain might also do so, at GM discretion.

Unholy Repair.name: Unholy Repair
Unholy Repair.description: The locus is sustained by the power of Tar-Baphon. The orb regains 10 Hit Points at the beginning of every round and is repaired by void damage.

Ankhrav.name: Ankhrav
Ankhrav.publicNotes:
These horse-sized, burrowing monsters generally avoid heavily settled areas like cities, but ankhravs' predilection for livestock and humanoid flesh ensures that the creatures do not remain in the deep wilderness for long. Desperate farmers whose fields become infested by ankhravs often have little recourse but to seek the aid of adventurers.

Ankhravs are immense, burrowing, and insectile predators, considered by inhabitants of the rural areas of the world to be an all-too-common plague.

Armor-Rending Bite.name: Armor-Rending Bite
Armor-Rending Bite.description: The ankhrav makes a mandibles Strike; if the Strike hits, the target's armor takes the damage and the acid damage bypasses the armor's Hardness.

Spray Acid.name: Spray Acid
Spray Acid.description:
Frequency once per hour

Effect The ankhrav spews acid in a @Template[cone|distance:30], dealing @Damage[3d6[acid],1d6[persistent,acid]|options:area-damage]{3d6 acid damage and 1d6 persistent acid damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|options:area-effect] save).

Deadhand Spell.name: Deadhand Spell
Deadhand Spell.description: Death Tower necromancers have crawling hand familiars. They can cast a spell with a range of touch and command the hands to deliver the spell; the crawling hand uses its 2 actions for the round to move to a target of the necromancer's choice and touch that target.

Drain Bonded Item.name: Drain Bonded Item
Drain Bonded Item.description:
Frequency once per day

Requirements The necromancer hasn't acted yet on this turn

Effect The necromancer expends the power stored in their bonded staff. During the current turn, they cast one spell they prepared today and already cast, without spending a spell slot. They must still Cast the Spell and meet the spell's other requirements.

Give a Hand.name: Give a Hand
Give a Hand.description: A Death Tower necromancer's familiar can use its actions to retrieve an item of light or negligible Bulk they're wearing and place it into one of their free hands.

Quickened Casting.name: Quickened Casting
Quickened Casting.description: If the necromancer's next action is to cast a spell or cantrip of 3rd rank or lower, they reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action).

Curse of the Destroyer's Bloodlust.name: Curse of the Destroyer's Bloodlust
Curse of the Destroyer's Bloodlust.description: After a turn where the oracle of Zagresh doesn't make a Strike, she takes a –2 status penalty to her AC and saving throws until she makes another Strike. During this penalty, she gains a +2 status bonus to her Strikes and fast healing 5.

Destructive Touch.name: Destructive Touch
Destructive Touch.description:
Requirements Zagresh's Gaze is active

Effect The oracle's hands and tusks ooze acid, making a soft sizzling noise and dealing an additional 1d6 acid damage to her unarmed attacks.

Zagresh's Gaze.name: Zagresh's Gaze
Zagresh's Gaze.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect The oracle of Zagresh throws aside her weapons and focuses on the pure divine power of destruction. She attempts a @Check[will|dc:27] save. On a success, she gains access to her claw and jaws Strikes. This effect lasts [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

One Eye Phantom.name: One Eye Phantom
One Eye Phantom.publicNotes: When the One Eye Hold betrayed Diskah Night Watcher, the dying shaman's anguish rippled out and bound the spirits of the traitors to the Ethereal Plane.

Walk the Ethereal Line.name: Walk the Ethereal Line
Walk the Ethereal Line.description: The One Eye phantom walks the thin line between the Ethereal Plane and the Universe in order to exist on both planes simultaneously. They can shift back to solely on the Ethereal Plane by using this ability again.

Atonement for the Damned.name: Atonement for the Damned
Atonement for the Damned.description: PCs might try to calm the phantoms instead of destroying them. At the GM's discretion, the PCs may attempt skill checks to talk down the spirits. On a success, the phantom's AC lowers by 2 as they become more willing to move on from their shame and dishonor, and they might even waver in attacking the PCs. Spells or rituals like @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.7Fd4lxozd11MQ55N]{Atone} that require penitent creatures have no effect on these phantoms.

Ectoplasmic Bleed.name: Ectoplasmic Bleed
Ectoplasmic Bleed.description: If a creature hits the phantom with a melee Strike, the creature is splashed with ectoplasm, taking @Damage[(2d4+7)[spirit]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). On a failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}.

Susceptible to Death.name: Susceptible to Death
Susceptible to Death.description: Though phantoms aren't alive, neither are they undead, and they are uniquely vulnerable to the effects of death. A phantom whose Hit Points are reduced to 0 as a result of a death effect (such as from a spell like @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Z9OrRXKgAPv6Hn5l]{Execute}) is immediately whisked away to the River of Souls, where their soul resumes the usual path to afterlife.

Phantom Touch.name: Phantom Touch
Phantom Touch.description: Each time they make a Strike, the One Eye phantom can choose to deal spirit damage instead of the normal physical damage type.

Skullcracker.name: Skullcracker
Skullcracker.description:
The One Eye phantom grabs the head of a creature as a jagged, spectral stone seeped with blood manifests before it. The One Eye phantom smashes the head of the creature against the stone with supernatural force, dealing @Damage[(2d12+7)[spirit]] damage with a @Check[will|dc:19] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature hallucinates that their skull has been fractured. They take full damage and are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}.

Critical Failure As failure, but double damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}.

Orc Hunter.name: Orc Hunter
Orc Hunter.publicNotes:
The elite hunters of an orc hold often operate alone, venturing into the wilderness of Belkzen for weeks at a time to track and bring down dangerous wildlife. They don't necessarily limit themselves to hunting animals, making them excellent bounty hunters, as their skills are just as useful against humanoids.

While some orc warriors specialize in a technique of their hold, others synthesize styles from all over into a unified yet versatile technique.

Hunting Trophies

Orc hunters frequently take bone fragments from megafauna or powerful opponents they slay as trophies. To show off their combat skill, they display these bones by inlaying them in armor or weapons, forming intricate patterns that provide an account of that orc's triumphs in battle for those who know how to read them.

Orc Duels

Dueling is a frequent practice among many cultures of Golarion, and orcs are no exception. The lethality of these duels varies based on the duel's impetus—a friendly bout goes to first blood or until a combatant is knocked out, while a fight in response to a vendetta or insult is more likely to the death. Unlike in many dueling traditions, spectators are encouraged to verbally aid combatants, shouting out encouragement to one's preferred fighter and invectives to their opponent.

Experienced Tracker.name: Experienced Tracker
Experienced Tracker.description: An orc hunter can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.EA5vuSgJfiHH7plD]{Track} while moving at full Speed.

Hunt Prey.name: Hunt Prey
Hunt Prey.description: The orc hunter designates a single creature as their prey. They must be able to see or hear the prey, or must be tracking the prey during exploration. They gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} their prey and a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.EA5vuSgJfiHH7plD]{Track} their prey. They also ignore the penalty for making ranged attacks within their second range increment against the prey. They can only have one creature designated as their prey at a time. If they use Hunt Prey against another creature, the prior creature loses the designation and the new prey gains the designation.

Hunter's Edge.name: Hunter's Edge
Hunter's Edge.description: The orc hunter deals an additional 1d8 precision damage the first time they hit their hunted prey in a round.

Back Pedal.name: Back Pedal
Back Pedal.description:
Trigger K'zaard is targeted by a Strike

Effect K'zaard moves unpredictably and suddenly as he tries to dodge. He gains a +1 circumstance bonus against the triggering attack. After the attack is resolved, K'zaard can Step.

Keep Talking.name: Keep Talking
Keep Talking.description: K'zaard continues to speak quickly and convincingly. Creatures @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened} by K'zaard's Weaseling Words can't reduce the frightened condition caused by that effect at the end of their next turn.

Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
Sneak Attack.description: K'zaard deals 2d6 extra precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

Weaseling Words.name: Weaseling Words
Weaseling Words.description:
K'zaard weaves together a short speech that gives the listener pause. Enemies within 30 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:24] save. While a creature is frightened by this ability, it's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to K'zaard.

Critical Success No effect, and the creature is temporarily immune to Weaseling Words for 1 minute.

Success The creature is unaffected.

Failure The creature is uncertain and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature is riddled with self-doubt about its own actions. It is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} and can't take hostile actions against K'zaard until the frightened condition ends.

Aurochs.name: Aurochs
Aurochs.publicNotes:
This massive bovine looks like a larger, wilder version of the domesticated cattle found throughout the Inner Sea region. During winter, vast herds of the animals roam the southern reaches of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords, but as the ice high in the mountains begins to melt, the aurochs migrate south into Belkzen, following their ancestral route along the Flood Road. Once the water flows in earnest and turns to massive floods, the sudden surge of greenery provides the aurochs with all the food they need to survive.

Aurochs are essential to life in Belkzen. Not only do the orcs there hunt the wild cattle for their meat, but they use every part of the aurochs, from hide to bones, to craft their weapons and tools. The aurochs' arrival prompts the beginning of the Flood Truce, during which the usually war-focused orc holds cease all hostilities to take advantage of this time of plenty.

While groups of 20 or fewer animals are sometimes encountered, most aurochs travel in herds that stretch to the horizon. The land thunders under the weight of these herds, and the dust kicked up by their hooves is often spotted days before the aurochs—or the floodwaters that spurred their migration—arrive.

Aurochs bulls stand between 6 and 7 feet tall at the shoulder and weigh around 2,500 pounds, although prodigious specimens can grow to over 7 feet tall and 3,000 pounds. Cows are smaller, standing near 6 feet at the shoulder and weighing up to 2,000 pounds. All aurochs have slightly curved horns that grow up to three feet long from base to spear-sharp tip.

The aurochs is represented in much of Belkzen's artwork. Not only are the animals vital to survival in that unforgiving land, but their strength, power, and tenacity serve as potent symbols for orc holds.

Aurochs Rolling

Farmers throughout the Inner Sea often joke about cow tipping: sneaking up on an unsuspecting bovine and pushing it over while it sleeps. But since cattle sleep lying down, the animal usually has the last laugh. Nonetheless, some younger orcs in Belkzen spend evenings creeping up on sleeping aurochs to roll them over. This usually results in little more than a startled aurochs.

Easily Spooked.name: Easily Spooked
Easily Spooked.description: The aurochs gains a +5-foot-status bonus to Speed while @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened}.

Safety in Numbers.name: Safety in Numbers
Safety in Numbers.description: Aurochs move together to keep safe from predators. While within 10 feet of another aurochs, the aurochs gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC.

Wild Charge.name: Wild Charge
Wild Charge.description: The aurochs Strides twice and makes a single horn Strike against any foe along its path.

Insulting Lesson.name: Insulting Lesson
Insulting Lesson.description:
Trigger A creature within 30 feet of Old Marrow critically fails at a skill check or attack roll

Effect Old Marrow quips with mocking advice. He Feints the triggering creature, even if the creature is not within Old Marrow's melee reach.

Lone Wolf.name: Lone Wolf
Lone Wolf.description: Old Marrow works alone and excels when attacking isolated prey. His Strikes against creatures who are not adjacent to any of their allies deal 1d6 additional precision damage.

Shoanti Ancestor Storm.name: Shoanti Ancestor Storm
Shoanti Ancestor Storm.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:26] to redirect the anger of the haunt back onto itself, @Check[intimidation|dc:28] to goad the Shadefield spirits into attacking someone outside of the ritual circle, or @Check[religion|dc:28] to assuage the spirits in the area. Four successes are needed to disable the haunt long enough for the ritual to be completed.
Shoanti Ancestor Storm.hazarddescription: Smoke and fire explode up around the boundaries of the storm, accusatory screams and threats of violence echoing over the winds and crackling of flames. Broken weapons and shields litter the ground and Shoanti warriors emerge from the smoke, blood running down their faces as they prepare for battle.
Shoanti Ancestor Storm.routine: (4 actions) The ancestor storm uses 1 action to release a pulse of void energy, restoring @Damage[10[healing]] Hit Points to each Shadefield spirit in the area, before using its next 2 actions to make an arrows of rage Strike against all non-Shoanti creatures within 60 feet of the cairn. The haunt then uses its 4th action to use Calling the Blood on one random leader participating in the ritual. The haunt loses an action for each Shadefield spirit killed, and when there are no Shadefield spirits remaining, the haunt uses its next action to summon a new Shadefield spirit into the battle.
Shoanti Ancestor Storm.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the Shadefields but resets immediately. The haunt is only permanently disabled when Diskah Night Watcher is put to rest. Otherwise, the haunt reforms within 24 hours of being Disabled.
Shoanti Ancestor Storm.stealthdetails: (trained) to notice the smoke intensifying and filling with the tortured faces of lost souls

Ancestors' Wrath.name: Ancestors' Wrath
Ancestors' Wrath.description:
Trigger The ritual begins

Effect The ancestor storm howls in fury and pain. This deals @Damage[(2d10+11)[spirit]] damage to all non-Shoanti creatures in the area, who must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Calling the Blood.name: Calling the Blood
Calling the Blood.description: The ancestor storm randomly chooses a Shoanti ritualist to distract. The ritualist must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by the voices of their ancestors for 1 round, increasing the DCs to Disable the haunt by 2 for the duration.

Arrows of Rage.name: Arrows of Rage
Arrows of Rage.description: The hazard makes an arrows of rage Strike against all non-Shoanti creatures within 60 feet of the cairn.

Blisterwell Oathrisen.name: Blisterwell Oathrisen
Blisterwell Oathrisen.publicNotes:
Most undead are reanimated without consent, but those known as oathrisen arise willingly—at least at first. These loyal subordinates swear themselves to a cause or leader, vowing to assist even in death. Within days of their death, their oath reanimates them and restores most of their memories. In theory, an oathrisen's existence lasts no more than a year. In practice, the cause's leader might demand their service indefinitely. If denied their afterlife too long, some oathrisen turn on their former masters, afterward either becoming independent or destroying themselves.

The oathrisen presented here is a variant that frequents Blisterwell during the events of this Adventure Path. Other regions and traditions might create oathrisen with different abilities.

Knight By Another Name

An oathrisen resembles a graveknight—both being heavily armored, corporeal, undead warriors. Yet a graveknight arises through its own unholy ambitions and is inextricably bound to its armor. By comparison, an oathrisen reanimates out of loyalty and is not intrinsically malicious. Some oathrisen even resent graveknights, taking special joy in destroying these unholy copycats.

Armored Disguise.name: Armored Disguise
Armored Disguise.description: A Blisterwell oathrisen's undead nature is not obvious. While wearing armor, an oathrisen gains a +4 circumstance bonus to its Deception checks to Impersonate a living orc and does not require a disguise kit to do so. Creatures that don't notice the disguise are not subject to the oathrisen's Frightful Presence. An oathrisen can abandon its disguise as a free action with the manipulate trait when it attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2u915NdUyQan6uKF]{Demoralize} a creature.

Drop the Mask.name: Drop the Mask
Drop the Mask.description: Once a Blisterwell oathrisen is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, portions of its armor break off. This reduces its AC by 2, increases its Speed by 5 feet, and gives it weakness 5 to vitality damage. This also immediately exposes nearby creatures to the oathrisen's frightful presence.

Curse of Whispers.name: Curse of Whispers
Curse of Whispers.description:
A creature muffled by this curse cannot speak above a whisper. The creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] save to use actions with the auditory trait

Saving Throw @Check[will|dc:25]

Stage 1 muffled for 1 minute (1 minute)

Stage 2 muffled for 1 minute (1 minute)

Stage 3 muffled for 1 day (1 day)

Wound Ripper.name: Wound Ripper
Wound Ripper.description: A Blisterwell oathrisen's Strikes against creatures with the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded} condition or taking persistent bleed damage deal 1d6 additional precision damage.

War Auroch.name: War Auroch
War Auroch.publicNotes:
War aurochs are especially large versions aurochs bred to pull war machines as their primary duty. Particularly aggressive calves are selected for additional training to fight in battle as support for warriors.

Belkzen's megafauna husbandry is a source of orc pride, with many species bred for everyday life. In battle, they provide an edge against foes.

War Barding

A war aurochs is often fitted with pieces of armor to make its attacks deadlier, such as spiked ornaments on their horns or sharp-toed hoof shoes. The symbol of a hold is painted directly onto the beast's hide and left there even if another hold captures it, where it's often treated as a prisoner of war.

Bellow.name: Bellow
Bellow.description: The war aurochs bellows aggressively, attempting a single Intimidation check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2u915NdUyQan6uKF]{Demoralize} against all enemies within 30 feet.

Stomp.name: Stomp
Stomp.description: The war aurochs strikes the ground with its hooves, causing tremors in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30]. Creatures in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:25] save or be knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Bull of Zagresh.name: Bull of Zagresh
Bull of Zagresh.publicNotes:
The fierce bull of Zagresh is an enormous, easily riled bovine with sharp horns like a stag's. The bulls are bred and raised for sport as well as war.

Belkzen's megafauna husbandry is a source of orc pride, with many species bred for everyday life. In battle, they provide an edge against foes.

Running of the Bulls.name: Running of the Bulls
Running of the Bulls.description: When a bull of Zagresh starts its turn adjacent to another bull of Zagresh, it gains a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed.

Berserk.name: Berserk
Berserk.description: If the bull has 40 or fewer HP at the start of its turn, it must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or go berserk. A berserk bull of Zagresh wildly attacks the nearest living creature, or the nearest object if no creatures are nearby.

Berserk Stampede.name: Berserk Stampede
Berserk Stampede.description:
Requirements The bull of Zagresh is berserk

Effect The bull of Zagresh Strides up to double its Speed and makes a horn Strike against up to three creatures with a –1 circumstance penalty. If it hits, it deals 1d6 extra damage and knocks the target @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Deathless Zealot of Zagresh.name: Deathless Zealot of Zagresh
Deathless Zealot of Zagresh.publicNotes:
Deathless zealots are powerful variants of deathless acolytes. They're as ambitious as deathless hierophants but more martially oriented, seeking out and slaying alleged heretics and enemies of their faith in pursuit of completing their divine missions. This zealot is dedicated to Zagresh, but any deity of warfare could create a deathless zealot. Due to their focus on battle and conflict, orc deities are especially likely to create deathless zealots in preference to other forms of deathless acolytes.

War's Toll

In the wake of the War of Immortals, deathless acolytes of all types have grown in number, especially deathless zealots, as both deities and their followers fell in battle without completing divine mandates. Curiously, deathless zealots of slain deities retain their power and continue to roam, still striving to ca rry out missions granted to them by their lost patrons.

Field of Slaughter.name: Field of Slaughter
Field of Slaughter.description: 40 feet. Allies within the aura treat their weapons and unarmed attacks as keen, even if the weapons don't meet the requirements. Critical hits within the aura deal 3d6 persistent bleed damage, in addition to any other critical hit effects. If the critical hit would already deal persistent bleed damage, increase the damage to 3d6+3.

Ravage Heretic.name: Ravage Heretic
Ravage Heretic.description:
Trigger A creature within 60 feet who the deathless zealot can see and who doesn't worship Zagresh casts a divine spell

Effect The deathless zealot attempts to counteract the triggering spell using their spell attack modifier and a counteract rank of 5. If the spell is counteracted, the caster is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 3} from the pain of the backlash. If the deathless zealot fails to counteract the spell, the caster is temporarily immune to further uses of this ability for 24 hours.

Spellsmash.name: Spellsmash
Spellsmash.description: The zealot Casts a Spell that requires a spell attack roll or has a range of touch, channeling it into their greatclub, then makes a melee Strike. This attack roll determines the effects of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks for their multiple attack penalty, but the penalty isn't applied until after the Spellsmash. The zealot can't use Spellsmash again until they recharge it with a single action, which has the concentrate trait.

Tooth Fairy Leader.name: Tooth Fairy Leader
Tooth Fairy Leader.publicNotes:
Lone fairies usually need several minutes of elbow grease and a sleeping or restrained subject to extract a tooth.

Tooth fairies spawn when a child's tooth (or, less commonly, an entire child) is buried in terrain rife with fey energies. Hatching from the buried teeth like larvae from an egg, tooth fairies build crude pliers from whatever they can find, then go hunting for more teeth—regardless of the owners' willingness.

Plaque Burst.name: Plaque Burst
Plaque Burst.description: When killed, a tooth fairy bursts into sticky, foul-smelling white dust. Each creature in a @Template[emanation|distance:5] must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure).

Tooth Tug.name: Tooth Tug
Tooth Tug.description:
Requirements The tooth fairy's last action was a successful pliers Strike against a creature with teeth

Effect The tooth fairy attempts a @Check[thievery|defense:fortitude] check against the creature's Fortitude DC, dealing @Damage[2[bleed]] damage on any result but a critical failure. On a critical success, it also pulls out one of the target's teeth.

If the creature loses a tooth, it takes a –1 status penalty to Charisma-based skill checks and must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] to Cast a Spell unless that spell has the subtle trait. These effects last for 1 day, or until the stolen tooth is returned and the target regains at least 1 Hit Point.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.4q8Of8NM9DC8kWyK]{Effect: Tooth Tug}
